races.name: Racing To School Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cushendun
runners.spotlight: 0-7 (0-4 over hurdles) but he went close over C&D (heavy) in December on his second hurdle start and again ran well when third at Ayr (2m4f, soft) recently; has shown promise on good to soft ground; leading claims.
runners.comment: 0-7 but has shown some good form over hurdles (including C&D) and has leading claims

runners.name: Wise Move
runners.spotlight: Four-time point winner who has run some good races in defeat in handicaps this season and was stretched by 3m last time; he's 0-11 under rules and exposed but is in with an each-way shout this afternoon.
runners.comment: 0-11 under rules and exposed but he's in with an each-way shout this afternoon

runners.name: CMon So
runners.spotlight: Former pointer who is with a respected stable and could be suited by the step up in trip on hurdle debut; however, he failed to threaten in either bumper and could do with settling better than last time.
runners.comment: Could be suited by step up in trip on hurdle debut but failed to threaten in either bumper

runners.name: Queens Secret
runners.spotlight: Won Irish mares' maiden point (3m, soft) last March on her second start and subsequently bought for Â£40,000; this is her first run since but she's in good hands and makes her rules debut in a weak race; interesting.
runners.comment: Point winner who was bought for Â£40,000 and has joined good yard for rules career
runners.owner: They Are Never At Home Partnership

runners.name: Maiori
runners.spotlight: Easily won Irish maiden point (3m, soft) in December on second start and showed some promise when fourth of 12 at 8-1 on recent hurdle debut at Ayr (2m, soft); he's a possible improver at this longer trip; one to consider.
runners.comment: 3m point winner; fourth over 2m on hurdle debut; possible improver at this longer trip
runners.owner: Up4b And Molly And Paul Willis

runners.name: Countryman Joe
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at big odds at Kelso in his two bumpers, the first last May (good) and the latest last month (heavy); now goes hurdling.
runners.comment: Tailed off at big odds at Kelso in his two bumpers (good/heavy); now goes hurdling

runners.name: Hourman
runners.spotlight: Very close fourth of four finishers on debut in bumper here (2m, good) in October and took a step forward at Ayr (2m, soft) recently on third hurdle start, when keeping on into sixth (jockey suspended for ten days for failing to take all reasonable and permissible measures to obtain the best possible placing); the step up in trip could be a positive move and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Kept on into sixth at Ayr and the step up in trip could be a positive move; not ruled out

runners.name: Bold Reflection
runners.spotlight: Placed in two points and won a bumper in 2022; absent since mid-division in Grade 3 mares' bumper at Punchestown in April 2023; it remains to be seen how much ability she retains but she's travelled from Ireland to make her comeback and it's a weak race; the betting may be informative.
runners.comment: Bumper winner in 2022; returns from very long absence but in weak race; betting may guide

runners.name: Eagles Cairn
runners.spotlight: Has shown some ability but a reproduction of his best form would prove insufficient today and he's been pulled up on his last two starts.
runners.comment: Has shown some ability but he's been pulled up on his last two starts

runners.name: Culzean
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in her two bumpers and tailed off at 100-1 on recent hurdle debut at Ayr (2m4f, soft).
runners.comment: Didn't show much in her two bumpers and tailed off at 100-1 on recent hurdle debut at Ayr

runners.name: Strathcatcha
runners.spotlight: Big odds and well beaten or pulled up on all five starts.
runners.comment: Big odds and well beaten or pulled up on all five starts
runners.owner: M Waterton D Amos G Rutherford

runners.name: Comedaygoday
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in 2022 but was then absent until this January when pulled up in handicap at Leicester (200-1); a tongue-tie now goes on; she may be best watched for now.
runners.comment: Showed promise in 2022 but was then absent until this January when pulled up at Leicester

runners.name: Colonel Jack
runners.spotlight: Pulled up at triple-figure odds over hurdles on his two starts (2m/2m4f, soft/good to soft).
runners.comment: Pulled up at triple-figure odds over hurdles on his two starts

races.name: Holiday At Hexham Racecourse Caravan Park Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Scottish King
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler but he's proving progressive over fences and landed handicaps (around 3m) at Market Rasen and Plumpton recently, showing a likeable attitude on latter occasion; up 2lb but he remains quite lightly raced and merits serious consideration in his hat-trick attempt.
runners.comment: On hat-trick after wins at Market Rasen and Plumpton; merits serious consideration again

runners.name: Killybegs Jet Lady
runners.spotlight: Irish point scorer; made a promising chasing debut when runner-up over C&D in August and ridden too aggressively when pulled up back over C&D in October; she's worth another chance after a break.
runners.comment: Second over C&D but ridden aggressively when pulled up there latest; worth another chance

runners.name: Lewa House
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in this event 12 months ago but he's yet to hit top form this term and came in a well-held sixth back hurdling in 2m4f Ayr handicap last month; still can't be totally discounted returning here off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Runner-up in this 12 months ago; yet to fire this term but not discounted back here

runners.name: Didntgotwenty
runners.spotlight: On the up since fitted with cheekpieces and he readily got off the mark in 2m4f handicap at Musselburgh last month; hiked up 9lb but he seems sure to go well if his stamina holds out over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Musselburgh latest; up 9lb but seems sure to go well if stamina holds out

runners.name: King Of Cong
runners.spotlight: Yet to score over hurdles or fences for his current yard and comes here having been well below par on last two starts, only tenth in 3m1f Catterick handicap chase in December; lots more is needed after a break.
runners.comment: Yet to score over hurdles or fences for current yard and well below par last two starts

runners.name: West Lawn
runners.spotlight: Yet to get her head in front over fences but she posted a good second of four in 3m1f Catterick handicap last month; both her hurdling wins came here too, so she's not out of things.
runners.comment: Good second at Catterick last month; not out of things in her bid for a maiden chase win

runners.name: Iturgeon Du Breuil
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden and he underwent wind surgery before pulled up in 3m2f Sedgefield handicap chase in January; not one to rely on.
runners.comment: Still a maiden and pulled up at Sedgefield in January; he's not one to rely on

runners.name: Worth The Walk
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 9yo who arrives in good form, runner-up to Scottish King in 3m Musselburgh handicap last month; this point winner needs considering despite being 2lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Good second at Musselburgh last month; needs considering despite being 2lb out of h'cap

runners.name: Rightbackatyou
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden hurdler who made mistakes when pulled up on chasing debut in 3m Ayr handicap last month; he needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Pulled up on his chasing debut at Ayr last month; needs to take a major step forward

runners.name: Blue Indigo
runners.spotlight: Maiden who hasn't proved easy to catch right over hurdles this term, beating one in 3m Ayr handicap 18 days ago; starts out over fences today and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Not easy to catch right over hurdles this term; goes chasing now and others appeal more

runners.name: Reliance Kid
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point but he has shown very little since sent over hurdles, beating one on his handicap debut at Newcastle (2m4f) in January; now goes chasing on the back of a wind operation.
runners.comment: Little show over hurdles; goes chasing on the back of wind surgery with plenty to prove

races.name: Weatherbys Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Smart Decision
runners.spotlight: A two-time winner (around 2m) either side of Christmas and he has continued in good nick, failing to stay when stepped up to 2m5f at Kelso last month; this C&D winner can bounce back.
runners.comment: Failed to stay in 2m5f Kelso handicap last month; this C&D winner can bounce back

runners.name: Hes Bresilian
runners.spotlight: Upwardly mobile hurdler who readily made it two wins in his last three starts in 2m1f handicap at Carlisle last month; has more to offer, so not taken lightly despite a 9lb weights hike.
runners.comment: Readily landed Carlisle handicap latest; not taken lightly despite a 9lb weights hike
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing & T S Ingham

runners.name: Eddie My Eagle
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each of his four runs over hurdles, following up a Plumpton novice success in January with a cosy win on handicap debut at Newbury (2m) last month; this strong-travelling sort is capable of better still; player.
runners.comment: Scored at Plumpton and Newbury last two runs; capable of better still, so he's a player

runners.name: That One
runners.spotlight: Signed off last spring with a C&D novice win but he's failed to go on in a light campaign this season, coming in last of nine at Newcastle (2m1f) last month; has something to prove now.
runners.comment: C&D novice winner but last of nine at Newcastle (2m1f) last month; something to prove now

runners.name: The Cistercian
runners.spotlight: Quick to get off the mark for his current yard at Leicester (2m) in December and he was still close up and running well when unseating rider two out on handicap debut at Haydock (2m4f) 20 days ago; firmly in the picture.
runners.comment: Running well when unseating 2 out on h'cap debut at Haydock latest; firmly in the picture

runners.name: Dees Getaway
runners.spotlight: Scored at Ayr (2m) in November on just her second run for current yard but well below par all three runs since, only 11th at Carlisle (2m1f) last month; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Scored at Ayr in November but well below par all three runs since; others are preferred

runners.name: Fromheretoeternity
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D scorer last term; raced only twice this winter and she shaped well under a patient ride when fourth of nine in 2m1f Newcastle handicap last time; interesting for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Dual C&D scorer who shaped well when fourth at Newcastle last month; interesting eased 1lb

runners.name: Caramelo Rapido
runners.spotlight: Advanced his form when getting off the mark at the tenth attempt in 2m Doncaster handicap 25 days ago; this demands another personal best off a 5lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Doncaster 25 days ago; this demands another personal best off 5lb higher

runners.name: Bust A Move
runners.spotlight: Signed off last season with a win in 2m Newcastle handicap hurdle but he did not fire in a pair of 2m handicaps at Wetherby and Huntingdon before Christmas; plenty to prove after a break.
runners.comment: Yet to fire in a pair of 2m handicaps before Christmas; has plenty to prove after a break

runners.name: Freddy Robinson
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways in 2m Market Rasen handicap hurdle in December; comes here on the back of a recent run on the Flat at Newcastle, so can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Scored at Market Rasen in December; had recent run on Flat, so can't be discounted

runners.name: Barrakilla Vage
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden hurdler; well-held fourth of six on yard debut in 2m4f maiden hurdle at Musselburgh in December latest; goes handicapping with lots more needed from him.
runners.comment: Well-held fourth on yard debut at Musselburgh in December; goes h'capping with more needed

runners.name: Goldwyn
runners.spotlight: Gained a breakthrough success at Wetherby (2m) in March 2024 for Mark Walford; very lightly raced since though and off 13 months before a well-held sixth for his new yard in 2m2f Haydock handicap 20 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Off 13 months before well-held sixth for new yard at Haydock 20 days ago

races.name: BK Racing Hexham Marathon Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Court At Slip
runners.spotlight: Without a win since December 2023 but this consistent sort has finished second at Catterick (3m1f/3m, soft) on his last two starts and ran well over 3m6f there in January 2024; each-way claims.
runners.comment: No win since December 2023 but arrives in good heart and holds each-way claims

runners.name: Planned Paradise
runners.spotlight: Exploited a reduced mark to win readily at Bangor (extended 3m5f, soft) four weeks ago; on last season's evidence a 9lb rise is unlikely to stop him if he can back up that latest performance; high on the list.
runners.comment: Exploited a reduced mark to win readily at Bangor last month; 9lb rise might not stop him

runners.name: Wal Bucks
runners.spotlight: Easily made all in first-time visor (retained since) at Kelso (3m2f, heavy) last month and again ran well when fourth of five at Haydock (3m4f, good to soft) since; looked at home over 3m6f earlier in the season; chance.
runners.comment: Won in first-time visor at Kelso last month and again ran well at Haydock since; chance

runners.name: Jo Coko
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced point winner who scored in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Uttoxeter (3m2f, soft) in January on his second chase/handicap start; open to further improvement and today's test could be an ideal fit.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced; won over 3m2f at Uttoxeter and today's test could be an ideal fit

runners.name: Shighness
runners.spotlight: She's struggled to get competitive since finishing a close second in this last year (good to soft) but is 7lb lower this time around and didn't run badly over hurdles at Sedgefield (3m3f, soft) last month; not written off.
runners.comment: Hasn't threatened since close second in this last year but 7lb lower this time around

runners.name: Rob Roy Macgregor
runners.spotlight: 0-16 since winning on debut in March 2023 and he's 5lb out of the handicap today; however, he's been hinting in chases at about 3m this year that today's extra test of stamina may be right up his street; interesting for stable that had an excellent weekend.
runners.comment: 0-15 over hurdles/fences but has hinted this stamina test could be right up his street

runners.name: Pinot Rouge
runners.spotlight: She has an unconvincing, 0-5 record over fences and also something to prove regarding her current form, having been tailed off back over hurdles four weeks ago; however, she likes Hexham and won in good style over hurdles here last June; 6lb lower today and might not be a forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Something to prove regarding fences and current form, but well treated and likes it here

runners.name: Karuma Grey
runners.spotlight: Very inconsistent this season but he ran okay when fifth on his stable debut at Ffos Las (3m, soft) last Monday, in the process shaping as though this marathon trip would be worth exploring; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Shaped on stable debut over 3m as though this marathon trip would be worth exploring

runners.name: Don Brocco
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in this in 2024 and third last year; worth a second look given his record in the race but this 10yo has been pulled up/tailed off on his three runs this season and a major turnaround is required from 5lb wrong.
runners.comment: Worth a second look given his good record in the race but he's struggled this season

runners.name: Gibberwell
runners.spotlight: His last two wins have come here but this 10yo has been pulled up on his last two starts and is 8lb wrong at the weights.
runners.comment: Last two wins have come here but pulled up on last two starts and he's 8lb wrong today

runners.name: Marown
runners.spotlight: 12yo who is without a win since 2021 and is going beyond 3m1f for the first time; however, having dropped down the weights, he took a step back in the right direction when third over Carlisle's stiff 3m recently and his trainer does very well with veterans; not discounted.
runners.comment: 12yo who is without a win since 2021 but he's not discounted off a reduced mark

races.name: Ronnie Parkes 80th Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: So Many Roads
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Ayr (2m4f) in January but failed to back it up when a well-held fifth in another 2m4f handicap at that track 43 days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Won at Ayr in January but he failed to back it up there since; others appeal more

runners.name: Another High Five
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced sort who took a big step forward on his handicap debut when a running-on second of eight at Kelso (2m6f) 40 days ago; this Irish point second merits serious consideration nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Big step forward with Kelso second latest; merits serious consideration nudged up 1lb

runners.name: Getaway Master
runners.spotlight: Opened his account at Carlisle (2m3f) in November on just second run for Micky Hammond but his more recent form is far less encouraging, pulled up at Chepstow (2m3f) 40 days ago; rare hood goes back on.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Chepstow last time; rare hood goes back on; lots to prove

runners.name: Gardener
runners.spotlight: Ended his time with Olly Murphy under a cloud but back on track for his new handler and he posted a solid third of eight in 2m3f handicap at Catterick last month; considered from an easing mark.
runners.comment: Solid third of eight in handicap at Catterick last month; considered from an easing mark
runners.owner: Mrs J M Plummer & M E Sowersby

runners.name: Pure Sirloin
runners.spotlight: Not discredited when seventh of 13 in 3m handicap at Ayr 55 days ago; needs to build on it though if he's to end a lengthy losing sequence.
runners.comment: Not discredited when seventh in 3m h'cap at Ayr 55 days ago; needs to build on it though

runners.name: Yapper Hill
runners.spotlight: Posted his best effort when fourth of eight in 2m handicap hurdle at Wetherby last month; remains low mileage, so is not out of things from a slipping mark.
runners.comment: Best run when fourth at Wetherby last month; not ruled out from a slipping mark

runners.name: Do Ye Wanna
runners.spotlight: Off nine months but recorded his best effort when third of nine in 2m3f handicap at Carlisle 37 days ago; in the mix eased 1lb here.
runners.comment: Off nine months but recorded his best effort when third at Carlisle latest; in the mix

runners.name: Drop Kick
runners.spotlight: Proving really reliable after going in on his handicap debut at Catterick (2m3f) in November, coming in a very good third of 15 in 2m4f handicap at Newcastle 32 days ago; ought to be in the shake-up once more.
runners.comment: Reliable type and a very good third of 15 at Newcastle latest; ought to be in the shake-up

runners.name: Less Legacy
runners.spotlight: Captured this prize last year (fourth win at the track) and caught the eye when eighth of 12 in 2m4f handicap at Sedgefield 34 days ago, racing wide and set a lot to do; he's a player off a 2lb lower mark now.
runners.comment: Captured this last year; caught eye with Sedgefield eighth latest; player off 2lb lower

runners.name: Cold Henry
runners.spotlight: Ridden from the front when gaining a breakthrough win over hurdles in 3m1f Catterick handicap 21 days ago; this fair winning stayer on the Flat can make his presence felt despite a 5lb rise.
runners.comment: Breakthrough win at Catterick 21 days ago; can make his presence felt despite 5lb rise

runners.name: Chosen Hero
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence and offered little in two runs for her new handler, pulled up in 2m4f handicap at Leicester in January; now tongue tied first time; easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Pulled up in handicap at Leicester latest; tongue tied first time; easy to look elsewhere

runners.name: Dartmhor
runners.spotlight: Last of seven in 2m4f handicap at Sedgefield 15 days ago; very hard to make a case for in his bid for a maiden win from 4lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Last of seven at Sedgefield 15 days ago; very hard to make a case for in bid for first win

runners.name: LAllier
runners.spotlight: Had some fair form for Michael Wigham but well held in 2m handicaps at Uttoxeter and Fakenham this term after a long absence; hard to make a case for in his bid to gain a maiden success.
runners.comment: Well held after a long absence in two runs for his new yard; hard to make a case for

runners.name: Knacker Trapper
runners.spotlight: His sole success came in May 2024 but he comes here on the back of a solid recent third of 15 in 2m4f handicap at Newcastle; possibilities.
runners.comment: Comes here on back of a solid recent third of 15 in handicap at Newcastle; possibilities

runners.name: Our Laura B
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence and she was pulled up in 2m4f handicap hurdle at Newcastle 32 days ago; she's not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Pulled up in handicap hurdle at Newcastle 32 days ago; she's not easy to make a case for

runners.name: Half Track
runners.spotlight: Yet to show much spark this term and he was pulled up in handicap at Newcastle (2m7f) 57 days ago; cheekpieces and tongue-tie (last worn four starts ago) are tried again now.
runners.comment: Pulled up in handicap at Newcastle 57 days ago; cheekpieces/tongue-tie tried again now

races.name: Weatherbys Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Chapel Green
runners.spotlight: Hard to fancy on what he's shown on his last three starts but the step back up in trip may help and he's 1lb lower than when winning over 2m5f at Kelso in September; not written off.
runners.comment: Return to form is needed but he's 1lb below September's last winning mark; not written off

runners.name: Carrigeen Castle
runners.spotlight: 10yo who is winless since March 2024 and needs to improve upon what he's shown on his two outings this year (3m1f/2m7f, good to soft/soft); however, he was running well off 4lb higher when falling at the final fence at Wetherby (3m, soft) in November and might benefit from this shorter trip.
runners.comment: Hasn't really shone this year but on a reduced mark; might benefit from this shorter trip

runners.name: Casual Observer
runners.spotlight: Won at Ffos Las (2m4f, heavy) last February on second handicap hurdle start and it was his first run since when an encouraging second of four on chase debut at Bangor last month (2m4f, soft; albeit no match for the winner); open to improvement over fences and could play a leading role.
runners.comment: Encouraging second on last month's chase debut at Bangor and he's open to improvement
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate - Casual Ob

runners.name: Big John Wayne
runners.spotlight: Following an absence this very lightly raced point winner has shown promise at Ayr this year on his first two chase starts (2m/2m4f, heavy/soft) and a win may well be imminent.
runners.comment: Promise this year on first two chase starts, after an absence; a win may well be imminent

runners.name: Glory And Honour
runners.spotlight: Won off today's mark over 1m7f here last June and he's fine at today's trip; however, this 10yo has been below his best since that course win and a return to form is needed on his first outing since October.
runners.comment: Won off today's mark here last June but below best since; has had a break since October

runners.name: Ned Tanner
runners.spotlight: He's down to a dangerous mark and showed some spark at Haydock in December three starts ago; however, two disappointing performances have followed from this 10yo and he needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark and showed spark in December but two disappointing runs since

races.name: Horslyx - For Horse's Health & Welfare Mares' Open Maiden NH Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)

runners.name: Myfriendcooper
runners.spotlight: Won an Irish point in April 2024; tongue tied and returned from 21-month absence with an encouraging fourth of seven in Ayr bumper in January; open to progress.
runners.comment: Won Irish point; off 21 months before encouraging Ayr bumper fourth; can build on it

runners.name: Lady Bamburgh
runners.spotlight: Yeats mare who was brought down on her debut at Huntingdon but shaped with some encouragement when fourth of nine in a Kelso bumper last month; she may do better still, so can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise when debut fourth in Kelso bumper; may do better still, so not dismissed

runners.name: Emiko
runners.spotlight: Bought for Â£75,000 after wide-margin winner of an Irish point in November; this Walk In the Park mare hails from a family of useful hurdles/chase winners, so rates an interesting newcomer for stable among the winners.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; rates an interesting newcomer for stable among the winners

runners.name: Imstrawberryblonde
runners.spotlight: 125-1, hinted at promise when fifth of eight in bumper at Catterick (good) on her debut three weeks ago; this daughter of Nathaniel can take a step forward now.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut fifth in Catterick bumper; daughter of Nathaniel can take step forward

runners.name: Abbotrule
runners.spotlight: Maxios mare; dam, last in three bumpers, is a half-sister to 2m5f/3m3f hurdle winner Katy Royal; a considered newcomer for her in-form yard.
runners.comment: Maxios mare; a considered newcomer for her in-form yard

runners.name: Bredas Pearl
runners.spotlight: 1,500gns yearling, Â£13,000 3yo; Postponed mare; half-sister to winners Donizetti (6f/7f Flat; RPR 80), Cwynar (1m Flat; RPR 70) and Sir Westminster (Czech 2m1f hurdle); dam, poor maiden, is a half-sister to useful 7f winner Gymnaste; this Irish challenger can't be discounted, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: By Postponed; Irish raider who can't be ruled out, especially if market vibes are positive

runners.name: Lady Bam
runners.spotlight: Getaway mare who was purchased for Â£35,000 after winning an Irish point in February; she must enter calculations on her rules debut.
runners.comment: Getaway mare; purchased for Â£35,000 after winning an Irish point; must enter calculations

runners.name: Little Ruby
runners.spotlight: 150-1, beat one in Southwell bumper on her debut 35 days ago; this daughter of Mondialiste needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Beat one in Southwell bumper on her debut 35 days ago; needs to take major step forward

races.name: Always Gamble Responsibly At BetMGM Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Fort Augustus
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden but he's on a reduced mark and was runner-up over C&D last month; did clear best of the closers in that race and has claims if he can repeat that form.
runners.comment: 0-16 but he was a clear second off this mark over C&D last time; dangerous

runners.name: Kondratiev Wave
runners.spotlight: Ended a lengthy losing run when deat-heating over C&D (7-4 favourite) four weeks ago; up 2lb but he battled well last time and he's a reliable type who has form figures of 336231 since December; respected.
runners.comment: Ended a lengthy losing run when dead-heating over C&D last month; respected off 2lb higher

runners.name: Alashos
runners.spotlight: Sole win was over 6f on turf in July 2024; on reduced mark and was a creditable fourth at Kempton (6f, Polytrack) three weeks ago but that took his record to 1-22 and he's untried at this trip; others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Good fourth at Kempton latest but he's now 1-22 and is untried at this trip

runners.name: Semser
runners.spotlight: All of his five AW wins have been at longer trips but he went close over C&D last summer and was runner-up over 1m at Chelmsford last month; on same mark as for that last run and has possibilities if he gets a good pace and some luck; reapplied blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: All wins have been at longer trips but has possibilities if he gets a good pace to aim at

runners.name: Blue Collar Lad
runners.spotlight: Clearcut win in first-time blinkers on turf here (7f, soft) last June; several creditable efforts since but he was disappointing at Bath in his final run last autumn; has bit to prove with headgear missing after five months and this is competitive for the grade.
runners.comment: Has bit to prove after five months off and this is competitive for the grade

runners.name: Tilsworth Silver
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-16 and he's finished ahead of only four rivals in his last five starts; passed over.
runners.comment: Record of 0-16 and he's finished ahead of only four rivals in his last five starts

runners.name: Twitch
runners.spotlight: Returned after 139 days off with fair third in classified event at Kempton (7f) three weeks ago but this is tougher back in a handicap and his last win was in September 2024; others preferred.
runners.comment: Fair third in classified event last time but this is tougher and his last win was in 2024
runners.owner: The Limes Of Epsom Racing Club

runners.name: Raqraaq
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been gained over 1m at this track; last success was off 8lb higher last January but he's been generally disappointing since and was beaten 7l at Kempton (1m) three weeks ago; has a change of headgear but he looks opposable back in trip.
runners.comment: Dual course winner (1m) but he's hard to predict and was beaten 7l at Kempton last time

runners.name: Global Warning
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is on a losing run but he was finishing his race off strongly when second over C&D last month; a 2lb rise doesn't handicap him out of things and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: On long losing run but he finished well when close second over C&D last month; shortlisted

races.name: Always Gamble Responsibly At BetMGM Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Tilsworth Max
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 26 starts but that came over C&D in January and he finished well to go close in a classified event here (7f) last time; should get a decent tow into this race and he's in the mix back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in January and was close third here last time; dangerous back in a handicap

runners.name: Miss Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Ended a long losing run when dead-heating with Kondratiev Wave (runs in first division) over C&D last month; up 2lb but she had some traffic issues at a crucial stage last time and that was only her second at this trip; interesting contender
runners.comment: Unexposed over 7f and she forced a dead-heat over C&D last time; interesting contender

runners.name: Gundogan
runners.spotlight: Effective at 6f and 7f but his sole win was in a Carlisle novice in August 2024; went close off this mark at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) last month but was never involved when a remote fourth there last time; that was behind a handicap blot but he needs to step up again after that.
runners.comment: Sole win was in August 2024 and he flopped at Southwell last time; risks attached

runners.name: Manton Road
runners.spotlight: Showed signs of a revival back from a break when fifth on stable debut at Kempton (1m) three weeks ago; was beaten less than 3l in that race and he's now 8lb lower than for his win at Newcastle (7f) last spring; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: 4yo who showed signs of a revival on recent stable debut at Kempton; one to keep an eye on

runners.name: King Of The Dance
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with a creditable fourth at Wolverhampton (7f, Tapeta) last month and his stamina looked stretched when upped to 1m2f at Chelmsford last time; has possibilities on the drop back in trip and he has won here in the past.
runners.comment: Had excuse when upped to 1m2f last time and he's in the mix back in trip

runners.name: Havana Club
runners.spotlight: Won off 1lb lower at Wolverhampton (7f) in December but things have not gone well there in his last three runs; now steps back up in trip but he failed to beat a rival last time and he needs a major revival.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in December but things have not gone well there in last three runs

runners.name: So Chic
runners.spotlight: Came late when scoring over 1m here (AW) last August; on same mark as for that success but she's been quiet this winter and has some work to do back at this trip.
runners.comment: On last winning mark but she's been quiet this winter and others are preferred

runners.name: Taskheer
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner who has finished placed in his last four runs including when third in a classified event at Southwell (1m) in January; should be fine on this drop back in trip and he's respected back from a short break.
runners.comment: Dual course winner who has finished placed in his last four runs; respected

runners.name: Maury
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden who has yet to be placed and was beaten 11l here in his final run last year; looks opposable back from 141 days off.
runners.comment: 11-race maiden who has yet to be placed; opposable back from 141 days off

races.name: Midnite Are Upping The Betting Game Handicap

runners.name: Tortured Soul
runners.spotlight: All of his ten wins have been on AW and he had a near-miss over 1m3f at Kempton last Wednesday; on same mark here and he's a big player if he can repeat that form.
runners.comment: Ten-time AW winner who had a near-miss off this mark at Kempton last week; respected

runners.name: Robbo
runners.spotlight: On same mark as for his Goodwood win (1m) for Gary and Josh Moore last summer; made a low-key start for new yard last month but he returned to form with a creditable third here (1m2f) last time; was strong at the finish in that race and he's in the mix at this new trip.
runners.comment: Finished well when third here (1m2f) last time and he's in the mix at this new trip

runners.name: Upepo
runners.spotlight: Turned things around with a win over C&D in December but he's been well held in three subsequent runs; ran into some trouble behind an improver at Chelmsford (1m6f) last time but he's hard to predict and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in December but he's been well held in three runs since; risks attached

runners.name: Analogical
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip in two of his first four handicaps and he had an excuse when upped to 2m on slow ground at Doncaster last July; been absent since but he still has potential and needs a close look back in trip on his return; reapplied cheekpieces replace blinkers.
runners.comment: 2-5 in handicaps and he had an excuse over 2m last time; needs close look on his comeback

runners.name: Id Go Maniac
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who won a handicap at Wolverhampton (1m4f) in November but his form has nosedived in last two starts; now drops back to this trip but he has a patchy profile and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in November but his form has nosedived in last two starts

runners.name: Naval Tribute
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in three bumpers in early 2025; has had four Flat runs this year and he made the breakthrough on handicap debut at Kempton (1m3f; cheekpieces added) three weeks ago; up 3lb for that narrow success but he was strong at the finish and is open to more progress over this longer trip; strongly respected.
runners.comment: Won at Kempton and he's open to more progress on this step up to 1m4f; key player

runners.name: Provision
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who struggled on his hurdling debut in December but he showed some promise on AW last autumn including when third in a Newcastle novice (1m2f); returns to the Flat after another break but he needs checking in market on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo who returns to the Flat and needs watching in market on handicap debut

runners.name: Yaa Min
runners.spotlight: Well held in his four runs for Roger Varian and latest was over 1m4f at Wolverhampton in December; changed hands for 8,000gns since; still unexposed and now goes handicapping but needs a transformation on stable debut.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but he needs a transformation on handicap/stable debut

races.name: Daily Profit Boosts At betmgm.co.uk Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Thecoffeepoddotco
runners.spotlight: Took a while to open her account but is a dual 5f course winner this year; also suited by 6f but current mark demands a career-best effort and some of her 3yo rivals have more potential.
runners.comment: Dual 5f course winner this year; 6f also suits but some of the 3yos have more potential

runners.name: Space Bear
runners.spotlight: Did not kick on after winning 7f Wolverhampton on last summer's debut for Marco Botti but her three 6f handicap runs during the winter were all very respectable and, following a Â£26,000gns sale, it will be interesting to see what Michael Appleby can do with her.
runners.comment: All three handicap runs for Marco Botti were very respectable; now with new stable

runners.name: Welcome Retreat
runners.spotlight: Went very close off 1lb lower mark on last month's handicap debut at Southwell (6f) and was not discredited when third in Wolverhampton novice a fortnight ago; also suited by Polytrack and probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Went close on last month's handicap debut and not discredited when third in recent novice

runners.name: Filly Foden
runners.spotlight: 0-13 as a 2yo for Alice Haynes but 2-5 for new stable this year, the latter win gained by over 4l in 5f course handicap last month; flopped badly off her new mark at Wolverhampton ten days later but should be okay back at 6f and has possibilities if back on song.
runners.comment: Flopped badly last time but won twice over 5f here beforehand; player if back on song

runners.name: Miss Lady Grace
runners.spotlight: Consistent filly who was given good tactical ride to win 7f Wolverhampton maiden from the front last month, her fifth start; not obviously well treated for this first handicap, though, and may not enjoy the luxury of an uncontested lead this time.
runners.comment: Dominated tactical 7f maiden from the front last month; more is needed today
runners.owner: R H J Martin And Mrs J Martin

runners.name: Autumn Affair
runners.spotlight: Displayed clear ability when making the frame in all three qualifying races, perhaps most notably when second of 12 at Southwell (6f) on second occasion; should have some improvement to come in handicaps; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Displayed clear ability in all three qualifying races and can improve in handicaps

runners.name: Whiskey Kisses
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with respectable fourth of seven in 5f Southwell maiden this month and has now made the frame on the last four of her five starts; needs to build on that recent effort here but the return to 6f will suit; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Shaped quite well over 5f this month, after a break, and this return to 6f will suit

races.name: Midnite, Building The Future Of Betting Classified Stakes (Div I)

runners.name: Beaune
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-17 but his win was over C&D last month and he backed that up with a second behind a clearcut winner at Southwell (1m4f) 11 days ago; remains unexposed at this trip and he's respected back in a classified event.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last month and he backed that up with a second at Southwell; respected

runners.name: Pablo Prince
runners.spotlight: Triple C&D winner who came from off the pace to go close in a Chelmsford handicap (1m2f) last month; now drops back into a classified event and he's a big player back up in trip.
runners.comment: Triple C&D winner who went close over 1m2f at Chelmsford last time; big player

runners.name: Zooks
runners.spotlight: Just 2-36 and has been disappointing since her second in a Wolverhampton handicap (1m6f) in December; ran into a handicap blot when third at Southwell last time but she was beaten 12l and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Just 2-36 and has been disappointing since her second at Wolverhampton in December

runners.name: Belle Of Kt
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who made the breakthrough when defying a wide draw in classified event at Wolverhampton (1m4f) two weeks ago; that was a brave win over a clear second and she's open to more progress back on Polytrack; key player.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton (1m4f) two weeks ago and she's open to more progress; key player

runners.name: Spaceage Love Song
runners.spotlight: Delivered what he'd long threatened at Wolverhampton early last month in a 0-50; well held in two starts since but he met traffic on his penultimate run and he failed to settle at Southwell (1m6f) last time; comes with risks attached but he's not ruled out back in trip.
runners.comment: Won over 1m4f at Wolverhampton last month and has claims if he can recapture that form
runners.owner: M L W Bell Racing Ltd

runners.name: Nymphaea
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but she's been placed in last two starts including when a close second in a Southwell handicap (1m6f) 12 days ago; looks open to more progress on this drop back in trip and she's an interesting contender.
runners.comment: 0-8 but was a close second at Southwell last time and she's respected back in trip

runners.name: Arenas Del Tiempo
runners.spotlight: Won a Chelmsford handicap (13.3f) last summer but she's not hit the same level since and was beaten 8l at Wolverhampton (2m) three weeks ago; that took her AW record to 1-26 and others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Record of 1-26 on AW and she's not easy to predict; down the list
runners.owner: D Edmonston T Helliwell & S J Holden

runners.name: My O My
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo but she's not finished closer than 8l to a winner; tongue-tie is now added but this looks tough on these terms and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo but she's not finished closer than 8l to a winner; passed over

runners.name: Tara Iti
runners.spotlight: Eight-time hurdle winner but he's been out sorts for his current yard and was tailed off in a claiming hurdle at Catterick three weeks ago; has some questions to answer back on the Flat and his sole win in this sphere was in 2020.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a claiming hurdle last time and his sole Flat win was in 2020

runners.name: Everest
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden but she's been placed in a bumper and two hurdles; still unexposed on the Flat and she ran well to finish third in a classified event here (1m2f) last month; has claims if she can build on that and this step up in trip looks a positive.
runners.comment: Unexposed on the Flat and was placed in a classified event here (1m2f) latest; in the mix
runners.owner: Alan Jamieson Site Services & G L Moore

runners.name: Keeper Of Secrets
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has yet to be placed; ran respectably in a classified event at Wolverhampton (9.4f; cheekpieces added) last week but he needs improvement on this step back up in trip.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden who has yet to be placed and this looks tough at the weights

runners.name: Sky Dancer
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on consecutive AW starts (1m3f) in February 2024 but lightly raced since and she's struggled for current stable; record is now 0-13 and she has a lot to prove after 193 days off.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden who has struggled for current yard and has a lot to prove on her return

races.name: Midnite, Building The Future Of Betting Classified Stakes (Div II)

runners.name: Ignition
runners.spotlight: 0-15 but he's reached the frame in his last three starts including over 1m4f and 1m6f at Wolverhampton in last two runs; didn't get the best of runs in a handicap last time and he should go well again back in trip.
runners.comment: 0-15 but he's reached the frame in last three runs and should go well again back in trip

runners.name: Saachi
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose performer who got off the mark at the 19th attempt when landing classified event at Wolverhampton (1m4f, Tapeta) two weeks ago; got an easy lead but he justified support in good style and they were strung out behind him; key player.
runners.comment: Made the breakthrough with a clearcut win at Wolverhampton two weeks ago; key player

runners.name: Gearings Point
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who enjoyed a productive 2023; losing run up to 18, but she was placed in a handicap over C&D last month and has each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Losing run is up to 18 but she was placed in a C&D handicap last time; each-way claims

runners.name: Tatmeen
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Kempton (1m4f) last May but he's been regressive since and was tailed off over C&D last time; record is now 1-12 and he needs a big turnaround.
runners.comment: Won at Kempton last May but he's been regressive since and needs a big turnaround

runners.name: Alex The Great
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come over further on the AW; drops to this level for the first time but he's struggled since last April and needs a major revival in new headgear.
runners.comment: All three wins have come over further and he's struggled since last April; new headgear

runners.name: Pure Theory
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden who still has stamina to prove at this trip and she was a long way below form at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last week; opposable.
runners.comment: 16-race maiden who was a long way below form at Wolverhampton last week; opposable

runners.name: Dotties Star
runners.spotlight: His sole win was over hurdles 14 months ago but he looked a bit unlucky when runner-up in that sphere at Huntingdon (2m) 24 days ago; now returns to the Flat but conditions should be fine and he needs a close look in the market.
runners.comment: Looked a bit unlucky over hurdles last time and he's in the mix back on the Flat

runners.name: Change Of Fortune
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 35 starts and he finished eighth of nine in a classified event at Wolverhampton (9.4f) last month; remains unexposed over this trip but he's hard to predict and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Just one win from 35 starts and he was in rear at Wolverhampton last time

runners.name: Foinix
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Brighton (1m4f, soft) last September but he's been out of sorts under both codes since including a remote fifth over hurdles at Plumpton last week; blinkers now return but he's 1-30 on AW and has plenty to prove back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Just 1-30 on AW and he's been out of sorts under both codes since October; opposable
runners.owner: M Robinson Jack Pook & Jeanette May

runners.name: Paps Of Jura
runners.spotlight: Struggled in his first five runs but he showed signs of ability when a staying-on fifth in Wolverhampton handicap (8.6f) two weeks ago; looks worth a try at this new trip but he's drawn out wide and this is a tough task on the figures.
runners.comment: Still unexposed but he's drawn out wide and this looks tough on the figures

runners.name: Warrior Lion
runners.spotlight: 29-race maiden who has struggled back over hurdles in his last two runs; finished second in a classified event at Wolverhampton (1m4f) in January but was beaten 6l and that took his AW record to 0-12; down the list.
runners.comment: 29-race maiden who has struggled back over hurdles in last two starts; down the list

runners.name: Onlyriversrunfree
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has not finished closer than 6l to a winner including in a 1m2f handicap here in December; cheekpieces are now added and she needs improvement at this new trip after a break.
runners.comment: Well held in all six runs and she needs improvement at this new trip; headgear on

races.name: Join The Midnite Movement Handicap

runners.name: Profit Street
runners.spotlight: Won three over 7f here of late and looked a bit unlucky not to make it four last Friday, when having Way To Dubai just behind; the drop to 6f shouldn't hurt and his claims are obvious off the same mark.
runners.comment: Won three over 7f here of late; the drop to 6f shouldn't hurt and his claims are obvious

runners.name: Way To Dubai
runners.spotlight: Course regular who's hard to win with and done most racing in recent times over further (stays 1m fine); whether he'll have the toe for this is the question.
runners.comment: Course regular who's hard to win with and done most racing in recent times over further

runners.name: Due Date
runners.spotlight: All victories have come in a visor, never off a mark this high, and has always been happiest at Chelmsford; not in any form in two runs back from a short break and would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Not in any form in two runs back from a short break and would be a surprise winner
runners.owner: A Bangs & Miss J A Challen

runners.name: Beaming Light
runners.spotlight: Landed a hat-trick last summer, latterly off a 4lb lower mark, all over 1m; excuses last time and had previously been in good form in the eyeshield but this will be more about speed on his first ever run in a sprint.
runners.comment: Excuses last time and had previously been in good form; first ever run in a sprint

runners.name: Desdemona
runners.spotlight: Taken advantage of a sliding mark lately, with two wins in lesser company, latterly for Ryan Kavanagh; only gone beyond 5f once and she'll find this company tougher as she chases a hat-trick upped another 5lb.
runners.comment: Only gone beyond 5f once and she'll find this company tougher upped another 5lb

runners.name: Lequinto
runners.spotlight: Veteran who's better known for his turf exploits these days, landing a quickfire Windsor hat-trick for Matthew Lloyd Slater last June; otherwise has been running fairly of late and is back on his last successful mark.
runners.comment: Better known for turf work these days; running fairly and back on his last winning mark

runners.name: Phoenix Moon
runners.spotlight: Hard to knock a record over C&D reading 1313214 and Rossa Ryan is a positive booking back from five months off, despite being 0-6 for the yard (won with their last two runners); however, stall 11 isn't ideal for one happiest on the sharp end.
runners.comment: Hard to knock a record over C&D reading 1313214 but stall 11 isn't ideal on comeback

runners.name: Sandscreendeliverd
runners.spotlight: Only gone beyond 5f twice, not looking to see out 6f on either occasion (both first time out); otherwise made a pleasing comeback from a similarly wide stall here 39 days ago and is entitled to improve.
runners.comment: Only gone beyond 5f twice, not looking to see out 6f on either occasion; fair comeback run

runners.name: Fai Fai
runners.spotlight: Remains generally consistent but is moderate these days and there's nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh; this a bit tougher than he's used to on his return from a layoff.
runners.comment: Nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh; this a bit tougher than he's used to

runners.name: Mart
runners.spotlight: Two wins and several near-misses to his name over C&D; had plenty of chances off a sliding mark of late and will need the breaks if dropped in as anticipated from the outside stall.
runners.comment: Two wins and several near-misses over C&D; plenty of chances off a sliding mark of late

runners.name: Style King
runners.spotlight: Made most when winning off 3lb lower over C&D last autumn; ran well first time out last spring and the hood is in place back from five months off, so there are reasons for optimism.
runners.comment: Ran well first time out last spring and the hood is in place; reasons for optimism then

runners.name: Northcliff
runners.spotlight: Landed a turf hat-trick for the visor first going on last spring (won twice in May the year before too); well held in two AW runs from wide stalls this winter and can do better at this lower level with the visor back in place.
runners.comment: Landed a turf hat-trick last spring; can do better at this lower level with the visor back

races.name: BetMGM Supports Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Comprador
runners.spotlight: Brother to the 5f/6f 2yo winner Trillium (including Group 2); made a promising start over C&D in January and he found improvement with another close third at Newcastle (6f) last month; that was an odds-on defeat but he sets a good standard on that form and is strongly respected with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Close third in his two runs this year and he sets a good standard; key player

runners.name: Magic Lady Mae
runners.spotlight: Made a low-key start at Kempton (6f) last October but she left that form behind when a front-running third at Chelmsford (7f) two weeks ago; needs to build on that but she has a good pedigree and is open to more progress on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Front-running third at Chelmsford 13 days ago and she's open to more progress back in trip

runners.name: Shavkat
runners.spotlight: Brother to 6f Group 3 winner Cold Case; won at 14-1 on C&D debut last June and he ran into a smart prospect when an odds-on third at Doncaster three weeks later; left Roger Varian for 20,000gns since; returns after a lengthy absence but he should have more to offer this year and he's respected back at this track.
runners.comment: Won on C&D debut last June and was placed at Doncaster next time; respected on his return

runners.name: Caviar Cowboy
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability in both runs this winter, most recently when second of five in a Kempton maiden (6f, Polytrack) last month; still early days but this looks a tough assignment on the figures and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Has shown ability in two runs this winter but this looks tough on the figures

runners.name: Westport
runners.spotlight: 120,000gns foal, 270,000gns yearling; closely related to 5f-7f winner Burnt Sugar (including AW 2yo Group 3; RPR 112), half-brother to seven winners including Brown Sugar (5f-6f 2yo including AW Group 3; 110) and Vespasian (5f-7f; 101); dam 5f/6f winner (98); already gelded, but makes plenty of appeal on paper and stable has plenty of winners first time out.
runners.comment: 270,000gns yearling; lots to like on paper and he needs a close look on debut
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Jellystone Park
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns foal, 50,000gns yearling; second foal; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 73), half-sister to 7f Group 2 winner Tariq and useful 6.5f/7f winner Tariq Too, is granddaughter of 5f/6f 2yo winner; has good standard to aim at on debut but he needs checking in market.
runners.comment: 50,000gns yearling; has good standard to aim at on debut and market should guide

runners.name: Tainted Love
runners.spotlight: Beaten around 11l in two sprint runs last month, with RPRs in the 40s on both occasions; remains best watched for now.
runners.comment: Beaten around 11l in two sprint runs last month and he remains best watched for now

runners.name: Saddle Up
runners.spotlight: Struggled at Chelmsford (6f) and Southwell (5f) last month and he has a lot to find back up in trip; hood added.
runners.comment: Struggled in two runs last month and he has a lot to find back up in trip; hood added

runners.name: Rainbow Sorbet
runners.spotlight: Made some late gains when third of eight at Gowran Park (1m, good; 10-1) last May but she was beaten 9l on that debut run; now switches to sprinting for new yard and she needs plenty of progress after 308 days off.
runners.comment: Some promise in sole run (6f) in Ireland but she needs plenty of progress on comeback

races.name: Midnite A Next Generation Betting App Classified Stakes

runners.name: Al Shabab
runners.spotlight: Currently 1-34 and the fact he's been beaten at 7-2 or shorter 11 times tells it own story; capable at this level but pulled too hard at Wolverhampton last Tuesday and has often taken a grip in recent times.
runners.comment: Currently 1-34 and beaten at 7-2 or shorter 11 times; has to settle better than last time

runners.name: Kaaress
runners.spotlight: Two of these behind when springing a surprise over C&D in January (first-time visor, switched from cheekpieces); not as good since and is some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Two of these behind when springing a surprise over C&D in January; not as good since

runners.name: Haveagobeau
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a generally consistent winter for Jack Dace over C&D; usually dropped in and a lot will hinge on how he settles early back from six weeks off, and how the race unfolds.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a generally consistent winter for Jack Dace over C&D; holds claims

runners.name: Desiderata
runners.spotlight: Not the easiest and plenty of short-priced defeats to his name but justified good support when under Billy Loughnane for the first time at Wolverhampton (4-7 for the yard) last Monday; has only run once on Polytrack but will rightly be popular under a penalty.
runners.comment: Not the easiest but justified good support under Billy Loughnane last week; leading claims

runners.name: Monks Mead
runners.spotlight: Goes well round here (AW and turf), including when just holding off Iftikhaar in a weak affair over C&D in January; continued in form since and is more likely than most at this level to give his running; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Goes well here; more likely than most at this level to give his running; each-way shout

runners.name: Celtic John
runners.spotlight: Well suited by switchback tracks and is 1-1 in this company following a 1m win here in January; suffered an overreach over C&D next time but still has stamina to prove back up in trip after an ordinary effort at Kempton (1m) latest.
runners.comment: Well suited by switchback tracks and is 1-1 in this company; capable if stamina holds

runners.name: Hijo De La Luna
runners.spotlight: Finally off the mark when beating Desiderata at Wolverhampton a month ago; always in the right place in a messy affair (first-time visor) that night and history suggests he's no good thing to reproduce the effort.
runners.comment: Beat Desiderata at Wolverhampton a month ago (always in the right place in a messy affair)

runners.name: Myna
runners.spotlight: Best of those to race on the pace at Newcastle two weeks ago; excuses for his only below-par effort this year (C&D) and goes on the shortlist under Rossa Ryan.
runners.comment: Excuses for his only below-par effort this year (C&D); on the shortlist under Rossa Ryan

runners.name: Spirit Charmer
runners.spotlight: In good form of late and again looked worth another try at this longer trip when just behind Monks Mead over 1m here four weeks ago; had plenty of chances now though.
runners.comment: In good form of late and again looked worth another try at this longer trip last time

runners.name: Angels Call
runners.spotlight: Former sprinter who finished well behind Desiderata back from a break last Monday; never gone beyond an extended 1m, so stamina is an obvious concern.
runners.comment: Former sprinter; never gone beyond an extended 1m, so stamina is an obvious concern

runners.name: Iftikhaar
runners.spotlight: A few near-misses to his name over C&D down the years; the drop back to 1m didn't work in his favour here last time and he holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Drop back to 1m didn't work in his favour here last time and he holds each-way claims

runners.name: Celebrating Ethel
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent mare who responded well to second-time cheekpieces when winning for Rossa Ryan (rides stablemate Myna) on Tapeta early last week (has a penalty for that); yet to finish anywhere near after seven runs on Polytrack.
runners.comment: Inconsistent mare; yet to finish anywhere near after seven runs on Polytrack

races.name: Hereford Easter Monday Family Raceday Handicap Chase

runners.name: Line Of Descent
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner who is well handicapped on old form and returned from long absence with fairly encouraging run in February; only third best on merit when well-held second of five at Doncaster (2m3f, good) later that month but still considered here.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on old form; yet to hit top gear since a long layoff but still considered

runners.name: Chief Black Robe
runners.spotlight: Needs to rebound quickly from a poor hurdle run 16 days ago but last month's second over fences at Market Rasen (2m3f, soft) was a pretty good effort and the forecast faster ground here ought to suit him better; big player if first-time visor has desired effect.
runners.comment: Didn't fire over hurdles last time but ran well in his latest chase; visor now added

runners.name: Ultra Viers
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly when last of four finishers at Leicester (2m4f, good to soft; just behind Jack Doyen) this month, after a break, and will be suited by this forecast good ground but he's 0-23 under rules and remains difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly when fourth at Leicester this month but is 0-23 under rules now

runners.name: Lelant
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with good-ground C&D win in October and followed up at Market Rasen (2m5f, good to soft) a few weeks later; struggled on heavy ground at Fontwell in December but has been freshened up again since and Sean Bowen keeps the ride; must be considered.
runners.comment: Won twice (once over C&D) in the autumn; subsequent defeat on heavy ground excusable

runners.name: Jack Doyen
runners.spotlight: On the downgrade now and yet to add to his win in 2024 edition of this race (soft ground) but returns here after two respectable runs, the latter a third at Leicester (2m4f, good to soft) 19 days ago, and should be fine on this quicker surface; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Yet to add to win in 2024 edition of this race but returns here after two respectable runs

runners.name: Fat Sam
runners.spotlight: Good-ground specialist who was useful in his prime for Chris Down; without a win since 2022, though, and recent point form for David Dennis is not inspiring (refused to race on latest appearance).
runners.comment: Useful in his prime but hasn't won since 2022; refused to race in a recent point

runners.name: Boys Of Wexford
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner; seemingly back in good nick when second at Fontwell (2m3f, good to soft) in November but ended 2025 with two non-completions and his record when fresh is not good; others preferred.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two non-completions and his record when fresh is not good

runners.name: Warners Cross
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on chasing debut in summer 2024 but returned from long absence with two disappointing hurdle runs towards end of last year; had wind op during latest 113-day break and now sports first-time cheekpieces; current ability hard to gauge.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two disappointing hurdle runs; had wind op during break; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Black Occ
runners.spotlight: 0-8 under rules; pulled up on chasing debut in January (wore cheekpieces) but ran quite well when third of six over hurdles here (3m1f, good; no headgear) this month and has possibilities if able to build on that effort back over fences.
runners.comment: Pulled up on chasing debut but has since run well over hurdles; a possible
runners.owner: P Conway T Lewis & G Wilson

races.name: CB Security & Protection Services Handicap Chase

runners.name: McGrath From Clune
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in November and ran well when second at Newcastle (2m, good to soft) 11 days later; looked the likeliest winner before unseating late at Taunton in December and now needs to bounce back from a disappointing run at Fontwell on Boxing Day (jumped right); tough mark but remains of interest with Sean Bowen booked.
runners.comment: Needs to bounce back from a modest run latest, but interesting with Sean Bowen booked.

runners.name: Chestnut Pete
runners.spotlight: Reliable veteran who was in good form when last seen, finishing third on his last three runs; won this off 1lb lower last year and he's worth considering back from a break.
runners.comment: Reliable 11yo who won this off 1lb lower last year; worth considering back from a break.

runners.name: Ballywood
runners.spotlight: Came very close to a first win in seven years when narrowly denied at Huntingdon (2m, good) seven days ago; can race keenly and unlikely to get an easy lead here, but worth considering if reproducing that effort off the same mark.
runners.comment: Narrowly denied a first win in seven years latest; strong claims if repeating that effort.

runners.name: Blackijo Dagrostis
runners.spotlight: 0-12 under rules but recent efforts over fences have been respectable, latest when a 3.25l third of six at Taunton (2m, good); back up 5lb which will likely make him vulnerable for the win, but he shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: 5lb rise might make him vulnerable for the win, but he shouldn't be far away.

runners.name: The Brickey Ranger
runners.spotlight: Struggled in handicaps for previous yard over a range of trips up to 3m2f this season since returning from a long absence, but capitalised on a reduced mark when winning over C&D last week on stable debut, ridden by Jess Stewart; carries a penalty but could figure if reproducing that effort.
runners.comment: Surprise winner over C&D last week; carries a penalty but could figure if in similar mood.

runners.name: Quinta Brigada
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three runs over hurdles (2m-2m3f) and finished a 38l fourth of five on last month's chase/handicap debut at Ffos Las (2m, heavy); may do better in time but others have more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Unexposed and may do better in time, but others have more pressing claims here.

races.name: Hereford Quarries Mares' "National Hunt" Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Isabella Islay
runners.spotlight: Much improved from her debut ninth when a running-on third of nine in 2m juvenile hurdle here last month; this Scalo filly should prove well suited by this longer distance; holds leading claims.
runners.comment: Shaped well when third here latest; should prove well suited by longer trip; big shout

runners.name: Millady
runners.spotlight: Nathaniel mare who shaped encouragingly when fifth of seven in juvenile at Wetherby (2m, soft) on her first go over hurdles last month; she can take a step forward now upped in trip; needs considering.
runners.comment: Encouraging Wetherby fifth last month; she can take a step forward up in trip; considered

runners.name: Johnnys Belle
runners.spotlight: Malinas mare who hinted at promise when third of five in a Ffos Las bumper (15-2) nine days ago; this winning pointer can take a step forward.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise with third in Ffos Las bumper latest; winning pointer; can build on it

runners.name: LAventara
runners.spotlight: Kayf Tara mare who is a three-time winner in points, latest in February; she must enter calculations now starting out in this sphere.
runners.comment: Kayf Tara mare; a three-time winner in points, latest in February; must enter calculations

runners.name: Kernie dAiry
runners.spotlight: A Â£155,000 purchase after winning a bumper at Durtal in November 2024 for Hugo Merienne; beat only one on her yard debut in Newbury Listed bumper 13 months ago but she's in very good hands so could still do better now going hurdling.
runners.comment: Won French bumper; tailed off in Newbury Listed bumper last February; may still do better

runners.name: Davita Blaze
runners.spotlight: Sent off 4-1 but she came in a well-held sixth of eight in bumper at Warwick on her debut 33 days ago; this Jet Away mare could take a step forward now going hurdling.
runners.comment: Well-held debut sixth in bumper at Warwick; could still take step forward now hurdling

runners.name: Tipperary Star
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful form shown when placed in bumpers in 2022; raced very infrequently since though and she came in a well-held ninth over 2m here in February before early fall at Huntingdon a week ago; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Fairly useful in bumpers in 2022; raced very infrequently since though and little promise

runners.name: Tierbay
runners.spotlight: 300-1, jumped ponderously when pulled up on her first go over hurdles in novice at Doncaster (2m3f, soft) in January; lots more is needed from this Frontiersman mare.
runners.comment: Pulled up on first go over hurdles at Doncaster; lots more is needed

runners.name: Svelte Silhouette
runners.spotlight: Fair form shown in bumpers but she's failed to show that since sent hurdling, only ninth of 13 in 1m7f Wincanton novice 20 days ago; not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Fair form shown in bumpers but she's failed to show that since sent hurdling

runners.name: Crystal Box
runners.spotlight: 80-1, always behind when 11th of 13 in novice here (2m, soft) on her debut last month; significantly more is required from this daughter of Crystal Ocean.
runners.comment: Always behind in novice here on debut; significantly more is required

races.name: Sunshine Radio Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Best Night
runners.spotlight: Produced a career best when winning at Huntingdon two-weeks ago back from a short break; faces a tougher task off 4lb higher, but she's open to further improvement, with conditions ideal.
runners.comment: Produced a career-best latest; tougher task off 4lb higher but still open to progress.

runners.name: Arnie Moon
runners.spotlight: Shaped encouragingly on his second hurdle start and ran creditably in a deeper maiden when sixth of 13 at Taunton (2m3f, good to soft) in January; despite top weight, he's worth a close look on handicap debut now back in trip, with Callum Pritchard's 3lb claim also a plus.
runners.comment: Not obviously thrown in on handicap debut, but recent efforts have been encouraging.

runners.name: Directly
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden hurdler, pulled up since a heavy defeat on January's handicap debut; drops back in trip and returns to better ground, with cheekpieces and a tongue-tie added, but has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Drops in trip and back on better ground, with new headgear, but has too much to prove.

runners.name: Madame De Labrunie
runners.spotlight: 1-8 in France, her sole win coming in a claimer; outclassed on British debut in a mares' Listed contest, and has failed to reproduce her French form in two runs in juveniles since; however, she's not too badly treated now switching to handicaps at an ordinary level and looks interesting.
runners.comment: Yet to reproduce her French form but makes handicap debut at an ordinary level; respected.

runners.name: Ramaah
runners.spotlight: 1m5f AW Flat winner who has only shown a modest level of ability in 11 runs over hurdles (2m-2m3f); latest when pulled up at Huntingdon (good to soft) this month; hard to recommend from out of the weights.
runners.comment: Pulled up latest and difficult to make a case for from out of the weights.

runners.name: Kotgar
runners.spotlight: Has failed to build on an encouraging reappearance at Ffos Las in October, finishing tailed off in November and pulled up the following month; absent since, but returns on a handy mark, albeit with something to prove.
runners.comment: Yet to build on an encouraging return but is on a handy mark.

runners.name: Inca
runners.spotlight: Finished second in an Irish Point in May 2023, but has failed to show any notable form in three runs over hurdles, most recently finishing tailed off at Market Rasen (2m2f, good to soft) in January; makes handicap debut.
runners.comment: Tailed off latest and needs this switch to handicaps to spark significant improvement.

races.name: Spa Motors Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Reel Orange
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles and out of sorts for previous stable on two runs following wind surgery, but came back to somewhere near best when well supported and finishing second on this month's stable debut at Exeter (2m2f, good to soft); leading candidate off the same mark.
runners.comment: Back to form on this month's stable debut and a leading candidate off an unchanged mark.
runners.owner: J M Dare T Hamlin J W Snook

runners.name: Land Girls Luck
runners.spotlight: Dual 2m4f hurdle winner last June and she's held her from well since, most recently back from a 151-day break when a 2.5l second at Hereford 18-days ago ridden by Aimee Jones; likely to make her presence felt again off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Has held her form well since winning last June and she's likely to make her presence felt.

runners.name: Miss Carbo
runners.spotlight: Has yet to build on her initial promise, albeit was highly tried on one occasion, but her pedigree suggests she may be suited by this longer trip and she's far from exposed; down 5lb and hard to dismiss with her age allowance, despite disappointing on this month's handicap debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo maiden who may be suited by this longer trip; worth considering.

runners.name: Midnight Ginger
runners.spotlight: Struggling for form this season, but last month's fourth at Warwick (2m3f, soft) following wind surgery was more encouraging, albeit beaten 18l; another step forward will be required, but she's on a good mark if doing so.
runners.comment: Recent run after wind surgery was more encouraging but she'll need to build on that.

runners.name: Broadway Honey
runners.spotlight: Showed glimpses of ability in maiden/novice company over hurdles (2m-2m6f; good/good to soft), but proved disappointing when switched to handicaps last time at Ludlow (2m5f); may do better in time but she doesn't appear to be particularly well treated despite being 4lb lower here.
runners.comment: Disappointed on last month's handicap debut and seems on a tough mark.

runners.name: Border Gem
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in any of her hurdles, latterly in two handicaps, shaping better than the bare result on both occasions, latest at Taunton (2m3f, good) in December when losing her pitch leaving the back straight before staying on again; one to consider off 3lb lower.
runners.comment: Shaped batter than the bare result on both starts in handicaps and one to consider.

runners.name: Miss Fedora
runners.spotlight: Signs of a revival when fourth at Fakenham (2m5f) in January, before finding 3m5f beyond her (refused at the final fence) on final start for Seamus Mullins; tongue tied but showed little when co-favourite on stable debut at Taunton yesterday; this would be her first hurdle race since 2024 were she to run again.
runners.comment: Tongue tied but showed little when co-favourite on stable debut at Taunton yesterday

runners.name: Hey Flint
runners.spotlight: Didn't really achieve too much in her qualifying runs and never figured when pulled up on her handicap debut over C&D (good) 18-days ago; remains on a tough mark despite the 3lb drop; cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Remains on a stiff enough mark despite the 3lb drop; cheekpieces now added.

races.name: Richard Layton Charity Day 16th April NH Flat Race (Amateur/Conditionals') (Cat 2 Elimination) (GBB)

runners.name: One Knight
runners.spotlight: Left behind an underwhelming debut when making all to win a Southwell (good to soft) bumper for new stable and ridden by Cameron Johnstone-Baker in December 2024, posting a useful RPR; key player if ready to roll after a 477-day absence.
runners.comment: Off since winning a Southwell bumper in December 2024; major player on return.

runners.name: Legend DAiry
runners.spotlight: 48,000euros 3yo, half-brother to useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Issar D'Airy; of obvious interest for his leading stable on debut, especially if strong in the market.
runners.comment: Half-brother to useful hurdle/chase winner Issar D'Airy; respected on debut; major yard.

runners.name: Great Oak Lad
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Felix Younger (bumper and high-class 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and My Valley (bumper, 1m4f-2m Flat and 2m hurdle); perhaps one for the longer term.
runners.comment: Dam related to a couple of winners but likely one for the longer term.

runners.name: Strong Man Tom
runners.spotlight: Â£7,000 3yo; sixth foal; brother to bumpers winners Peace Motivator and Peace Belle, half-brother to 2m/2m3f hurdle winner Simply Red; check the betting.
runners.comment: Â£7,000 3yo; brother to a couple of bumper winners; market best guide.

runners.name: Thunderous Sea
runners.spotlight: Flat-bred son of Night Of Thunder; half-brother to 1m winner Glencalvie, dam a half-sister to smart winners English Oak (7f/1m) and Forest Of Dean (1m2f Group 3); stable 0-36 in bumpers over the past five years and probably best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Flat-bred son of Night of Thunder who is probably best watched on debut.

runners.name: Mac Gayveur
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to several winners and displayed some ability when a 25l sixth of 11 at Southwell (soft) on last month's debut; one of two for the stable.
runners.comment: Half-brother to several winners; showed ability on last month's debut; may improve.

runners.name: Gentora
runners.spotlight: Sister to the useful 2m-3m hurdle/chase winner Masters Legacy; 20-1 when finishing a 26l fifth of eight at Warwick on heavy ground last month; others make more obvious appeal but she's another who may be suited by good ground; one of two for the stable
runners.comment: Sister to the useful Masters Legacy but she was well held on last month's debut.

runners.name: Mac J
runners.spotlight: First foal of a 2m6f hurdle winner; finished a 23l seventh of eight at Huntingdon (2m, soft) last month; possibly one for the further down the road.
runners.comment: Hinted at ability on last month's debut but possibly one for further down the road.

runners.name: Britway Belle
runners.spotlight: Won Irish mares' maiden point (3m, yielding) on debut in October and subsequently changed hands for Â£120,000 but beaten 33l on January's rules debut (soft, 12-1); more required but the better ground may help.
runners.comment: Irish mares' point winner last year, but beaten 33l on bumper/stable debut in January.

races.name: Virgin Bet Interactive Handicap

runners.name: Just Jump
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 19 starts was over 1m (soft) in Ireland last October; has mixed record since but he's not been with Tony Carroll for long and was a close third at Lingfield (6f, Polytrack) on his penultimate run; has possibilities if he can recapture that form and he's in the mix in new headgear.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but he went close at Lingfield on penultimate run; hood now tried

runners.name: Mighty Ruler
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 17 starts but that came off a similar mark here (7f) in January; no impact in last four runs but he ran well for a long way at Wolverhampton last time and is not ruled out on this drop back to 6f.
runners.comment: Won off similar mark here in January and he's not ruled out on this return to sprinting

runners.name: Vitalline
