runners.spotlight: Frequent slow starter who was always on the back foot over C&D last time but he justified favouritism here (7f classified) on his penultimate run; still looks feasibly treated on that form and has claims if this sets up for his closing style.
runners.comment: Frequent slow starter but he won over 7f here on his penultimate run; dangerous

runners.name: Horus
runners.spotlight: Sole win was in December 2024 but he finished close fourth over C&D in January, on his final run for Mike Murphy; been gelded since and he needs a close look on his return for new yard; visor goes on and tongue-tie and cheekpieces are removed.
runners.comment: Close fourth over C&D in his final run for Mike Murphy; needs a close look for new yard

runners.name: Rye
runners.spotlight: 0-13 but runner-up twice in a row at Wolverhampton in the autumn and she returned from a break with creditable third over C&D three weeks ago; had some of today's rivals behind her last time and she's a big payer if she can build on that run.
runners.comment: 0-13 but she returned from a break with a good third over C&D three weeks ago; respected

runners.name: Hes An Angel
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace to win over this trip at Wolverhampton in January; finished down the field in three subsequent runs but he had some traffic issues in a messy race at Southwell last time; now only 2lb higher than for his last success and has claims if he gets some luck.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in January and he had some traffic issues last time; dangerous

runners.name: Sub Thirteen
runners.spotlight: Four-time turf winner but he's 0-6 on AW and was well held over C&D on his stable debut three weeks ago; continues to drop down the weights but he's drawn out wide here and others are preferred; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: On reduced mark but he's 0-6 on AW and was well held over C&D on recent stable debut

runners.name: Valsharah
runners.spotlight: Free-going 4yo who is 0-19 and has been well held in two runs (7f/5f) for Mick Appleby this year; had a wide trip at Wolverhampton last time but he needs to raise his game on his step back up to 6f; hood removed and tongue-tie is now tried.
runners.comment: Record of 0-19 and has been well held in both runs for current yard; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Battle Point
runners.spotlight: Respectable efforts in classified events (5f) in last two runs but it's almost three years since his sole win and others are preferred; one of three runners for the yard.
runners.comment: Fair efforts over 5f in last two runs but it's almost three years since his sole success

runners.name: Haziym
runners.spotlight: Habitual slow starter who is 0-23 in Britain; perked up with a close third over C&D (20-1) in December but he's struggled in last two runs and others carry fewer risks.
runners.comment: Habitual slow starter who is 0-23 in Britain and has struggled in his last two starts

runners.name: Rory Rocket
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his second at Goodwood (6f, good) last August but he's lost his way in last four runs and was beaten 14l over C&D three weeks ago; record is now 0-10 and he has a lot to prove from tough draw.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden who has lost his way and was beaten 14l over C&D last time

races.name: Virgin Bet Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Unknown Journey
runners.spotlight: 21-race maiden; returned to form with a close second in classified event at Lingfield (1m) last month but he failed to back that up at Wolverhampton two weeks ago; hood is now added and he needs to bounce back again after that last run.
runners.comment: Close second at Lingfield last month but was well held last time and he's now 0-21

runners.name: Timely Affair
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won a classified event at Southwell (1m) in January but he didn't fire in a handicap there 16 days later; needs to step up again after that but he still has potential and could resume his progress after a short break.
runners.comment: Didn't fire latest but he still has potential and could resume his progress after a break

runners.name: Homme De Fer
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f at Wolverhampton and Lingfield in December and he's held his form with placed efforts in last three runs including a close call over C&D in January; still looks on a workable mark and he's strongly respected back up in trip.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 141333 since December and he's strongly respected back up in trip

runners.name: Aim For The Bull
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 20th attempt when landing a classified event at Southwell (7f) last month; ran right up to his best with that success but he's not easy to predict and was well held in a Southwell handicap two weeks ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Made the breakthrough at Southwell (7f) last month but he was well held there last time

runners.name: Daddy Chill
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo but he has a mixed record so far and was last of eight in a Lingfield handicap (1m) three weeks ago; dropped 5lb but he's looked a tricky customer in three runs for his current yard and has plenty to prove; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but he's struggled for current yard and has looked a tricky customer

runners.name: Resilient Lady
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who has yet to be placed and has been well held in classified events in her last three runs; opposable.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who has been well held in classified events in last three runs; opposable

runners.name: Whiskey Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but he finished well to go very close over C&D (11-2) in January; only 1lb higher after a short break and he's a big player if he can repeat that latest form.
runners.comment: Went very close over C&D in January and is a big player if he can repeat that form

runners.name: Petra Grey
runners.spotlight: Has not finished closer than fifth in her eight starts, including two handicaps and two classified events for current yard; made some late headway in blinkers here (7f) last time but she needs improvement back up in trip.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden who has not finished closer than fifth and she needs improvement

runners.name: Harry Dont Bite
runners.spotlight: Finished in rear at big prices in his three qualifying runs and same story on handicap debut at Lingfield (7f) last month; still early days but he needs a transformation back up in trip.
runners.comment: Has struggled in all four runs and he needs a transformation back up in trip

races.name: Virgin Bet Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Scenario
runners.spotlight: Won at Wolverhampton (9.4f) in January but that was a last-gasp success and she's not matched that form since; ran respectably when fourth at Southwell (1m) last time but she needs to find more back on Polytrack.
runners.comment: Last-gasp win at Wolverhampton in January but she's not matched that form since

runners.name: Dubai Harbour
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been over 1m2f at Lingfield (AW) but he gave it a good shot when runner-up over C&D three weeks ago; on unchanged mark and he should be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Both wins have been over 1m2f but he was runner-up over C&D last time; respected

runners.name: Volendam
runners.spotlight: 0-16 but she's finished runner-up in three of her last four starts including a close call at Southwell (1m) on Saturday; on same mark and she's a big player again back on Polytrack.
runners.comment: 0-16 but she went close off this mark at Southwell (1m) on Saturday; key player

runners.name: Whiteley Way
runners.spotlight: 19-race maiden but she's on a reduced mark and was beaten less than 2l when fifth at Wolverhampton (7f) last month; that was an eye-catching effort after a slow start and she's an interesting contender back up in trip.
runners.comment: 0-19 but was an eye-catcher at Wolverhampton and she looks interesting back up in trip

runners.name: Boujee Gold
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on her Brighton win last spring but she's 0-12 since and has been well held after slow starts on Tapeta (9.4f/1m) in last two runs; headgear is now removed and she needs to rediscover her spark.
runners.comment: Losing run is up to 12 and she's been well held after slow starts in her last two runs

runners.name: Port Noir
runners.spotlight: Got back in the groove when winning over C&D and at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last month; didn't fire in hat-trick bid at Wolverhampton three weeks ago but that was from a wide draw and she still looks on a workable mark; dangerous if things go more to plan this time.
runners.comment: Won over C&D and at Wolverhampton last month and she had an excuse last time; dangerous

runners.name: Grand Citadel
runners.spotlight: Won on turf (7f, good to soft) last spring but she's been lightly raced since and was beaten 17l at Southwell last month; now back on her winning mark but she needs a major revival back up in trip; blinkers reapplied.
runners.comment: Inconsistent mare who is 1-17 and was beaten 17l at Southwell last time

runners.name: Senseofentitlement
runners.spotlight: His sole win was in July 2023 and he's not finished closer than sixth in 13 runs since then; opposable.
runners.comment: Sole win was in July 2023 and he's not finished closer than sixth since then; opposable
runners.owner: Miss M P Bryant David & Eileen Bryant

runners.name: Purple Sky
runners.spotlight: Won at Down Royal (10.6f, good) last July but she's not come close to that form since and has been quiet in three runs for new yard this year; record is now 1-16 and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Won in Ireland last summer but she's been disappointing since and record is now 1-16

races.name: Virgin Bet Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Freda
runners.spotlight: Brings solid form, with her two Lingfield efforts (1m/7f) subsequently franked by the winners; beaten only half a length on debut; failed to show progress last time but may still do better.
runners.comment: Brings solid form, with her two Lingfield efforts subsequently franked by the winners

runners.name: Generic
runners.spotlight: 62,000euros foal; Kameko half-brother to French Group-placed 1m1f AW/10.5f turf winner Crohanne (RPR 103); dam 1m2f AW scorer (77), closely related to UAE 1m/9.5f dirt Group 2 winner Military Law; bred to take well to artificial surfaces; notable connections; interesting debutant.
runners.comment: 62,000euros foal; bred to take well to artificial surfaces; interesting debutant

runners.name: Miss Guinevere
runners.spotlight: 65,000euros yearling; by Camelot and first foal of 6f 2yo/1m winner (RPR 99); wears hood on debut; probably best watched unless the betting suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: 65,000euros yearling; wears hood on debut; probably best watched

runners.name: Midnight Media
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form, having finished last of 12 in 7f contest at Kempton; hood worn on that occasion is now removed.
runners.comment: Last of 12 in 7f contest at Kempton (wore hood; now removed)

runners.name: Enemy Agent
runners.spotlight: Earthlight half-brother to French winners Kind Of Kiss (1m2f; RPR 94) and Forelsket (1m/9.5f AW; 88); dam 7f AW/9.5f turf Listed scorer (101); in good hands and should be suited by the surface; shortlisted.
runners.comment: In good hands and, judged on breeding, should be suited by the surface; shortlisted

runners.name: Franschhoek
runners.spotlight: Saxon Warrior half-brother to three winners, notably Woodhay Wonder (multiple 6f; RPR 107); dam 7f/8.6f AW scorer (76), out of US 1m3f Grade 2 winner; check the betting.
runners.comment: Saxon Warrior half-brother to three winners; check the betting

runners.name: Eagle Thunder
runners.spotlight: Â£44,000 yearling; Sergei Prokofiev half-brother to 7f winner One Night Thunder (including AW; RPR 93), out of 6f AW scorer (91); this debutant is bred to act well on the surface; may show promise.
runners.comment: Â£44,000 yearling; bred to act well on the surface; may show promise

runners.name: Pace Attack
runners.spotlight: 36,000euros yearling, resold 50,000gns later in 2024; Australia half-brother to 7f winner Livio Milo (RPR 90); dam 6f/7f scorer (99); market may prove best guide as regards this Prescott newcomer.
runners.comment: Son of Australia; market may prove best guide as regards this Prescott newcomer

runners.name: The Bureau Club
runners.spotlight: Shaped with some promise, amid greenness, in 1m event at Lingfield; recorded only a modest RPR with that fourth of five but may do better with the experience under her belt.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise, amid greenness, in 1m event at Lingfield

runners.name: Pureis King
runners.spotlight: Flat-bred gelding who has raced three times in turf bumpers, failing to back up the form of his debut success; chance depends on how well he responds to the change of code, trip and surface.
runners.comment: Flat-bred gelding; raced three times in bumpers, failing to back up form of debut win

runners.name: Receipt
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two bumpers for Tom Ellis; has rejoined that trainer since faring a lot better (close second) in 7f AW Flat event at Southwell for Pam Sly; may build on that effort.
runners.comment: Down the field in bumpers; fared a lot better (close second) in 7f Flat event at Southwell

races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Thestral
runners.spotlight: Seemingly not straightforward as he's been gelded since Newcastle novice win (7f) and now goes in headgear; otherwise a nicely bred sort who may improve further.
runners.comment: Seemingly not straightforward but otherwise a nicely bred sort who may improve further

runners.name: Hamaleel
runners.spotlight: Has taken her form to a new level this year, making all over C&D then creditable second at Wolverhampton (8.5f); another easy lead would cement her respectable claims.
runners.comment: Improved form this year, making all over C&D then second at Wolverhampton

runners.name: Ebn Sabt
runners.spotlight: Contrasting performances since fitted with blinkers, winning at Lingfield (1m) then last of four at Southwell; mixed messages, with the blinds retained.
runners.comment: Contrasting performances since wearing blinkers; mixed messages

runners.name: Just A Gambler
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to prevailing narrowly at Chelmsford (1m) on handicap/seasonal debut; now 3lb higher in a deeper race but may be capable of further progress.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to prevailing narrowly at Chelmsford on handicap/seasonal debut

runners.name: Heart Sign
runners.spotlight: Has shown clear promise over 7f, while leaving the impression he'll be suited by this extra furlong on handicap debut and second start for new yard; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Leaves the impression he'll be suited by this new trip on handicap debut; interesting

runners.name: Armstrong
runners.spotlight: Disappointing on final start last summer (gelded since) but has otherwise shown some promise and this Boughey runner looks the type to improve now handicapping; one to note.
runners.comment: Boughey runner who looks the type to improve now handicapping; one to note

runners.name: Gnocchi
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival on handicap debut (6f, turf) when last seen and is best watched unless the market suggests a bigger effort is likely.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival on handicap debut when last seen

runners.name: Station Bar
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo season with a trio of second-place finishes over shorter; solid contender and may go one better over this new trip, being out of a multiple 1m winner for his connections.
runners.comment: Triple silver medallist who may earn gold over this new trip; dam multiple 1m winner

runners.name: Blues And Royals
runners.spotlight: Good third at Kempton (7f; AW debut) on final 2yo start; gelded since; may take another step forward kept to this venue on handicap debut; possibilities.
runners.comment: Good third at Kempton on final 2yo start; may take another step forward; possibilities
runners.owner: D Fish S Jones & J Barnett

races.name: Virgin Bet Novice Stakes (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Constitution Hill
runners.spotlight: Superstar hurdler on his day and one of the best-known horses in training; having developed serious jumping issues, he was switched to the Flat in Southwell contest (1m4f) last month and showed his class by producing an impressive performance that is backed up by the figures; sets a tall standard en route to Group targets.
runners.comment: Superstar hurdler on his day; impressive at Southwell on Flat debut; sets a tall standard

runners.name: Classical Allusion
runners.spotlight: Gained his Southwell 1m4f success six months ago in a time over three seconds slower than Constitution Hill recorded over the same C&D last month; however, this once-raced gelding is open to progress and, being so unexposed, looks one of the brighter opponents to the Henderson star.
runners.comment: Once-raced winner who is open to progress and looks one of the brighter contenders

runners.name: Roadlesstravelled
runners.spotlight: Useful, having shown best hurdles form with Grade 2 novice win; not in same league as Constitution Hill in that sphere and duly proved no threat to that rival at Southwell on Flat debut, finishing midfield; bagged a silver medal there (1m3f) since, however, and again has place claims.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler; runner-up at Southwell in latest Flat start; again has place claims

runners.name: Keep It Cool
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden hurdler; fared best of the rest behind easy winner at Wolverhampton (8.5f) last week on Flat debut, shaping as if this longer trip will suit; needs big improvement on form, however.
runners.comment: Modest maiden hurdler; runner-up at Wolverhampton last week on Flat debut

runners.name: Serviceman
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability (RPRs 70 and 69) in maidens over 1m on turf and 1m2f on AW in autumn 2024 for Owen Burrows; absent and gelded since; has joined Andrew Balding, who also runs Classical Allusion; possibly open to improvement; the betting may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: Showed some ability in autumn 2024 for Owen Burrows; heed the market signals

runners.name: Victors Spirit
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two bumpers but a faller in both attempts over hurdles; finished behind Roadlesstravelled at Southwell on return to the Flat, recording an ordinary RPR; plenty to find.
runners.comment: Finished behind Roadlesstravelled at Southwell on return to the Flat
runners.owner: The Ridgeway Racing For Fun Partnership

runners.name: Star Artist
runners.spotlight: Vastly inferior to Constitution Hill and Roadlesstravelled over hurdles; duly finished behind those two opponents at Southwell on the switch to this code; faces another stiff task.
runners.comment: Finished behind Constitution Hill and Roadlesstravelled at Southwell on Flat debut

runners.name: Edward Sexton
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who has perhaps found 7f/1m in adequate in his modest Flat attempts; may do better over this longer trip but handicaps are likely to be his ideal scene.
runners.comment: May do better over this trip but handicaps are likely to be his ideal scene

races.name: Virgin Bet Daily Price Boosts Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Sword Of Wessex
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after ten attempts but his rallying effort over 1m5f at Lingfield (second start for new yard) most recently suggests this new trip may suit; possibilities off reduced mark.
runners.comment: Still a maiden but latest effort suggests this new trip may suit; possibilities

runners.name: Fleurman
runners.spotlight: On a long losing spell, not the force of old and his record for current yard is far from solid; others preferred.
runners.comment: On a long losing spell and isn't the force of old

runners.name: Knight Templar
runners.spotlight: Took his C&D record to 2-2 with a success in January and posted a solid effort at Southwell in most recent Flat outing; took advantage of a handy hurdles mark since; respected.
runners.comment: Took advantage of handy hurdles mark last time; 2-2 over C&D; commands respect

runners.name: Naasma
runners.spotlight: Consistent mare who scored three times at Windsor (11.5f) last term and has posted two creditable efforts at Lingfield (latest over 2m) this year; C&D record reads 312; solid claims.
runners.comment: Consistent; creditable efforts at Lingfield this year; C&D record reads 312; solid

runners.name: High Point
runners.spotlight: Achieved little over hurdles the last twice; four-time winner on Flat turf but is less solid on AW form and doesn't seem set to defy top weight.
runners.comment: Four-time winner on turf but is less solid on AW form

races.name: Virgin Bet Daily Extra Places Handicap

runners.name: Mr Ubiquitous
runners.spotlight: Won four on turf last spring/summer (7f-1m), climbing the weights by more than a stone, before losing his way latterly; resumes 4lb above his highest successful mark, back from seven months off, but this is easier back on the AW (won his nursery here).
runners.comment: Resumes 4lb above his highest winning mark; this is easier back on AW (won nursery here)

runners.name: Safari Dream
runners.spotlight: Won three from the front over 6f early last summer, latterly off tonight's mark; Lewis Edmunds, who's often ridden him, partners Cypriot Diaspora, but he's gone well first time out more than once and the market will likely tell a tale.
runners.comment: Won three from the front over 6f last summer; gone well first time out more than once

runners.name: Cypriot Diaspora
runners.spotlight: Dual Ffos Las winner over this trip last August who resumes on a workable mark having lost her way latterly; nothing to suggest she's best caught fresh and she'll need to improve on previous AW efforts.
runners.comment: Nothing to suggest she's best caught fresh; will need to improve on previous AW efforts

runners.name: Mumayaz
runners.spotlight: Always needed things to fall just so given his run style; offered more at Wolverhampton last Monday, on his first run back with his old yard, and won with a visor first going on last autumn; first-time blinkers join his regular tongue-tie and quite capable if the race sets up for him.
runners.comment: Won for visor first going on last autumn (blinkers on); quite capable if the race sets up

runners.name: Mr Cool
runners.spotlight: Two turf wins last spring/summer came over 6f off higher marks; cheekpieces haven't helped this year and he's still to convince over this longer trip (needs to settle better than at Chelmsford last time).
runners.comment: Cheekpieces haven't helped this year and he's still to convince over this longer trip

runners.name: Terries Royale
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Michael Appleby came over a stiff 1m on soft ground; regressive since returning from a long absence but offered a bit more on his second run for this yard, who also saddle Mumayaz, last time and is well treated now.
runners.comment: More on his second run for this yard last time and is well treated now; 7f a bare minimum

races.name: Tuen Mun Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Smart Trio
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 3-7 in Australia, but out of the frame in all 16 local starts; also has stamina to prove; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: 3-7 in Australia, but out of the frame in all 16 local starts; hard to recommend
runners.owner: Peter Lee Kwok Wah Tony Lau Hon Kwan & Kenny Cheng Tsin Ki

runners.name: Mr Aladdin
runners.spotlight: Favourably treated on a few of his efforts last year and should appreciate the return to turf, but is now 0-32; needs to benefit from the change of headgear.
runners.comment: Now 0-32 and needs to benefit from the change of headgear

runners.name: Chateau Le Peche
runners.spotlight: 1-25, the win coming off 3lb higher at Sha Tin (1m2f) just under a year ago; only beaten just over a length into fifth over C&D in December and now 4lb lower, but he has the outside stall.
runners.comment: 1-25; not beaten far over C&D in December but he has the outside stall

runners.name: Rosewood Fleetfoot
runners.spotlight: Placed only once in 11 starts and two previous attempts over C&D leave him with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Placed once in 11 starts; two previous attempts over C&D leave him with plenty to prove
runners.owner: Lam Ka Lai & Yeung Wing Shut

runners.name: Perfect Pairing
runners.spotlight: 1-29 in Hong Kong, but has won once and finished runner-up three times from seven starts over C&D; needs a solid pace given his come-from-behind style, but a player if he gets it.
runners.comment: Won once and runner-up three times from seven starts over C&D; player granted a good pace
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Jerry Chow Shiu Ming

runners.name: Joyful Prosperity
runners.spotlight: 3-35 with all wins coming at Sha Tin (1m1f/1m2f) at the end of 2024; has not shown much lately, but the return to Class 5 level should help and he does stay this far.
runners.comment: 3-35; has not shown much lately, but the return to Class 5 level should help
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Silas S S Yang & Michelle H Y Yang

runners.name: Romantic Fantasy
runners.spotlight: 1-22 with the win coming over C&D at the start of 2024; beaten less than a length in his last three starts and is edging back up the weights despite not winning, but he again shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: C&D winner; beaten less than a length in last three starts; again shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Conspiracy Witness
runners.spotlight: Better effort when beaten just over 2l into fourth here (1m1f) three weeks ago, but now 0-13 and has stamina to prove over this far.
runners.comment: Better effort here three weeks ago, but now 0-13 and has stamina to prove over this far
runners.owner: Arthur Antonio Da Silva Betty Da Silva & Teresa Da Silva

runners.name: All Are Mine
runners.spotlight: 1-24 in Hong Kong, but has run well in his last two starts as when runner-up here (1m1f) three weeks ago; this trip appeared to stretch him in his only previous attempt, though.
runners.comment: 1-24 in Hong Kong; this trip appeared to stretch him in his only previous attempt

runners.name: Hailtothevictors
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed once in 12 starts and that was on dirt (1m); enough to prove on his first attempt over this far.
runners.comment: Placed once in 12 starts and that was on dirt; enough to prove over this far
runners.owner: Graham Kot Li Heng & Amy Chau

runners.name: Golden Fairy
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is 3-34, with one of his wins coming over C&D at the start of 2024; back off his last winning mark, but will need to put his recent efforts behind him.
runners.comment: C&D winner; back off his last winning mark, but will need to put recent efforts behind him
runners.owner: Rex Wong Siu Han Ivan Wong Siu Kei & Ann Wong Pui Gee

runners.name: Management Folks
runners.spotlight: 3-34 with his wins all coming here, two over this trip; drops into Class 5 company for the first time since 2022 and Zac Purton regains the ride; much respected in the returning cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; drops to Class 5 company for the first time since 2022; Zac Purton rides

runners.name: Kasa Papa
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck in a Class 4 here (1m1f) in November and ran with credit in one previous go over C&D, but well held back in a Class 5 last time and a record of 1-44 in Hong Kong is another concern; cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: Well held back in a Class 5 last time and a record of 1-44 in Hong Kong is another concern

runners.name: Super Hong Kong
runners.spotlight: Went up 8lb for his decisive win over C&D in November, but returned to winning form at Sha Tin (1m2f) in January; fourth in a recent trial here, but another 7lb rise will ask plenty of him.
runners.comment: C&D winner in November and at Sha Tin in January; another 7lb rise will ask plenty of him

races.name: Tsuen Wan Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf) (Div I)

runners.name: Kolachi
runners.spotlight: 1-3 in Ireland but 0-13 in Hong Kong; his effort over C&D three weeks ago when behind three of these didn't suggest a win was imminent.
runners.comment: 0-13 in Hong Kong and finished behind three of these over C&D last time

runners.name: Our Lucky Glory
runners.spotlight: All three wins in Hong Kong have come over 6f at this track, but he has made the frame on several occasions over C&D; well held at Sha Tin last time, but frame material again now returned here.
runners.comment: Has made the frame on several occasions over C&D; frame material again now returned here

runners.name: Northern Beast
runners.spotlight: 4-36 with all wins coming over 1m/1m1f here; continues to slide down the weights, but has become inconsistent and was behind four of these here three weeks ago; others more compelling.
runners.comment: Four-time winner here, but has become inconsistent and was behind four of these last time
runners.owner: Ngai Man Amy Wang Hong & Doris Ngai Yan Tung

runners.name: Take Action
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner; twice runner-up here (1m/1m1f) this year and didn't enjoy a clear run when behind a couple of these here three weeks ago; each-way claims granted a smoother passage.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; twice runner-up here this year; each-way claims

runners.name: Precision Hope
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in four starts over 6f/7f and anybody's guess how he will cope with this stiffer test.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in four starts; anybody's guess how he will cope with this stiffer test

runners.name: Yiu Cheung Victory
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D in November and at Sha Tin (1m2f) in January, but he's far too inconsistent to rely upon; others safer.
runners.comment: Won here in November and at Sha Tin in January, but well held in most of his other starts

runners.name: Super Goldendragon
runners.spotlight: Reserve; out of the frame in four starts over 1m1f/1m2f at Sha Tin; needs to improve for the change of track and switch to blinkers from cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in four starts at Sha Tin; needs to improve for new track; blinkers on
runners.owner: Yip Shu Bun & Jonathan Chok Shee Feng

runners.name: Prestige Superior
runners.spotlight: 2-6 in Australia, but no closer than tenth in six starts in Hong Kong including when dropped to Class 4 last time; best watched.
runners.comment: No closer than tenth in six starts in Hong Kong including when down to Class 4 last time

runners.name: Win Method
runners.spotlight: Form so far has been patchy, but he had no problem with the step up in trip when winning over C&D a fortnight ago; up 7lb, but remains unexposed over this far so shouldn't be taken lightly.
runners.comment: C&D winner a fortnight ago; up 7lb but remains unexposed over this far; not taken lightly

runners.name: Absolute Awakened
runners.spotlight: Sole win in ten starts came over C&D in December, but a 6lb rise seems to have anchored him in three starts since; 1lb drop not enough to tempt, especially from the outside stall.
runners.comment: Held three times since winning over C&D in December; has the outside stall

runners.name: Turin Warrior
runners.spotlight: All four wins over C&D, the latest off 2lb lower in November; has run well a couple of times from higher marks in the meantime, but he could have been better drawn.
runners.comment: All four wins over C&D, the latest off 2lb lower in November; could have been better drawn

runners.name: Ragga Bomb
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in Hong Kong with a convincing success over C&D in January and, despite a 9lb rise, was only beaten just over a length into third three weeks ago; respected.
runners.comment: C&D winner in January and third off this mark here three weeks ago; respected

runners.name: To Infinity
runners.spotlight: All seven wins over C&D, but never off a mark this high and he again hasn't enjoyed much luck with the draw.
runners.comment: All seven wins over C&D, but never off a mark this high; again draw high
runners.owner: Chan Wing Chiu & Yildiz Choi Yat Fan

runners.name: Vermilion Tempest
runners.spotlight: 1-1 in New Zealand, but well held in five starts over 6f-1m1f on turf/dirt in Hong Kong; needs plenty more.
runners.comment: 1-1 in New Zealand, but well held in five starts in Hong Kong; needs plenty more

races.name: Kwai Chung Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf) (Div I)

runners.name: Watch This One
runners.spotlight: 1-1 in Australia, but unplaced in all 14 Hong Kong starts; hasn't always enjoyed the best of luck in his races, but the draw hasn't been kind this time.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all 14 Hong Kong starts; the draw hasn't been kind this time

runners.name: Giddy Up
runners.spotlight: 1-24 in Hong Kong and recent form has been patchy despite a big slide in the weights; could also have been better drawn.
runners.comment: Recent form patchy despite a big slide in the weights; could have been better drawn

runners.name: Gameplayer Elite
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 3-3 in Brazil but took 19 attempts to break his duck in Hong Kong, which he finally did over C&D three weeks ago; the form has been franked, but a 6lb rise will demand more.
runners.comment: Off the mark in Hong Kong over C&D three weeks ago; 6lb rise will demand more
runners.owner: Lam Yau Pik Rex Lam Lam & Monica Lam Mung Ni

runners.name: Viva Boss
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in three starts and although not beaten far into sixth over C&D three weeks ago, is still some way down the list.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in three starts; some way down the list

runners.name: Golden Empire
runners.spotlight: Seven wins including three over C&D and, having dropped in the weights, he ran his best race for some time when a close third back here a fortnight ago; can make the running and a big effort is likely from the inside stall.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner and ran better last time; big effort likely from the inside stall

runners.name: Honest Witness
runners.spotlight: 0-11, but should appreciate the return to this trip having been placed three times over C&D; Zac Purton regains the ride, but a few of the others still hold stronger claims.
runners.comment: Placed three times over C&D but 0-11; others hold stronger claims
runners.owner: Arthur Antonio Da Silva Betty Da Silva & Teresa Da Silva

runners.name: Sunny Q
runners.spotlight: 13 starts at Sha Tin, winning one (6f) in October; still 5lb higher and will need to resume his progress on his first visit here.
runners.comment: Still 5lb higher than when winning at Sha Tin in November; first visit here

runners.name: Bits Superstar
runners.spotlight: Just 1-21 in Hong Kong, the win coming over C&D in May last year, but has finished runner-up off this mark in his last three starts here; a player once again, even though Zac Purton (who has been aboard for his last three outings) jumps ship.
runners.comment: C&D winner and has finished runner-up in his last three starts here; a player once again
runners.owner: Tam Jin Rong & Zhao Xiao Na

runners.name: Leading Agility
runners.spotlight: Three starts over 6f/7f at Sha Tin, not building on November's promising debut; needs the change of stable to bring about a resurgence.
runners.comment: Three starts at Sha Tin, not building on November's promising debut; new stable
runners.owner: Man Pui Kwan & Ma Pak Hung

runners.name: A Time For Us
runners.spotlight: 1-1 in Australia, but out of the frame in four starts at Sha Tin, the latest over 1m; drop to Class 4 one reason for optimism.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in four starts at Sha Tin; drop to Class 4 one reason for optimism

runners.name: Origin Of Form
runners.spotlight: 1-2 in Australia; drops in grade but hasn't beaten many in four starts in Hong Kong, and his wide stall won't make things easy.
runners.comment: Hasn't beaten many in four starts in Hong Kong; drops in grade, but drawn wide

runners.name: The Heir
runners.spotlight: 1-28 in Hong Kong, but has run very well to finish third over C&D in his last two starts; should go well again if in the same form.
runners.comment: 1-28 in Hong Kong but finished third over C&D in his last two starts; should go well again

runners.name: Tactical Command
runners.spotlight: Reserve; five wins including four over C&D, the latest off 1lb higher last July; runner-up here last month and his efforts either side can be excused on account of the draw; a possible if he gets a run.
runners.comment: Four wins over C&D, the latest off 1lb higher; a possible if he gets a run

runners.name: Amazing Victory
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at Sha Tin, but his success over C&D in first-time cheekpieces last June came out of nowhere; has a bit to prove on his first start since.
runners.comment: Win over C&D last June came out of nowhere; has a bit to prove on his first start since

races.name: Tsuen Wan Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf) (Div II)

runners.name: Leaping Star
runners.spotlight: 2-8 in Ireland, but hasn't beaten many in six starts in Hong Kong; plenty more needed, though this is a drop in class.
runners.comment: Hasn't beaten many in six starts in Hong Kong; plenty more needed, though drops in class
runners.owner: Maurice Lai Tai Hei John Lo Et Al

runners.name: Frantanck
runners.spotlight: 2-34 in Hong Kong with both wins coming at Sha Tin (1m), but has finished unplaced in his last 20 starts; can make the running.
runners.comment: 2-34 in Hong Kong; unplaced in his last 20 starts

runners.name: Harmony Galaxy
runners.spotlight: Both wins in Hong Kong have come over 1m1f here; 6lb below his last winning mark, but he has a bit to find with Star Elegance on latest course running (1m) and has the outside stall.
runners.comment: Has a bit to find with Star Elegance on latest course running and has the outside stall

runners.name: Beauty Viva
runners.spotlight: 2-7 in Britain but, despite a couple of near misses at Sha Tin, is 0-19 in Hong Kong; didn't run very well in one previous visit here (1m3f); enough to prove.
runners.comment: 0-19 in Hong Kong and didn't run very well in one previous visit here; enough to prove

runners.name: Excellent Boy
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in five starts over 6f-1m at Sha Tin, but not beaten far in his last two outings; better drawn this time and may have a bigger performance in him.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in five starts at Sha Tin, but not beaten far in his last two outings

runners.name: Super Sicario
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner including over 1m1f three weeks ago; both wins have come at Class 5 level, though, and the return to Class 4 may just find him out.
runners.comment: Dual course winner, but both at Class 5; return to Class 4 may just find him out
runners.owner: Tony Lau Yiu Tong & Thomas Lau Sum Fai

runners.name: Red Brick Warrior
runners.spotlight: Has finished out of the frame in four starts over 7f at Sha Tin, but shapes as though this longer trip may suit; still unexposed and worth a second look.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in four starts at Sha Tin, but shapes as though this longer trip may suit

runners.name: Star Elegance
runners.spotlight: No show in his first four starts at Sha Tin but, with blinkers added, he ran much better when beaten a neck over C&D a fortnight ago with two of these behind; up 2lb, but still open to further improvement and much respected.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck in first-time blinkers over C&D a fortnight ago; still open to improvement

runners.name: Another Zonda
runners.spotlight: 4lb lower than when winning a Class 3 over C&D last June, but not at his best lately and opposable from his draw.
runners.comment: 4lb lower than when winning over C&D last June, but not at his best lately; drawn wide

runners.name: Whowrotethatsong
runners.spotlight: Reserve; well held in three starts over 6f/7f; needs a lot more raised again in trip.
runners.comment: Well held in three starts over 6f/7f; needs a lot more raised again in trip

runners.name: Fortune Star
runners.spotlight: Went up 7lb after gaining his sole success over C&D in January; better drawn than when behind two of these a fortnight ago, but others still make more appeal.
runners.comment: C&D winner; better drawn than last time, but others still make more appeal
runners.owner: Kelvin Chan Kam Kei & Eddie Chan Kam Kong

runners.name: Glorious Journey
runners.spotlight: 2-26 in Hong Kong with both wins coming over C&D; nudged up another 2lb for his close second here three weeks ago and now 9lb above his last winning mark, but shortlisted nonetheless.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; 9lb above his last winning mark, but shortlisted nonetheless

runners.name: Celtic Times
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual C&D winner this season, but still 5lb higher than for the latest and is proving very inconsistent; others safer if he does get a run.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season, but still 5lb higher than for the latest; very inconsistent

runners.name: Dashing Pal
runners.spotlight: Both runs over 6f at Sha Tin a year ago, showing promise in the first but not in the second; has not shown much in his recent barrier trials and probably best watched for now.
runners.comment: Both runs at Sha Tin a year ago, showing promise in the first but not the second

races.name: Rotary Centenary Challenge Cup (Class 3) (Handicap) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Red Elegance
runners.spotlight: All five wins have come over 5f including four here, so his latest effort over 6f not hard to forgive; not at his best lately, but should get a nice tow into this from his good draw and has dropped 6lb below his last winning mark; may be worth chancing.
runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner now 6lb lower than when last successful; race may be run to suit

runners.name: Harmony N Blessed
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer whose ten wins include eight over 5f/6f here; 6lb lower than for the latest in November and most of his runs since have been over the longer trip; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Eight wins here including four over this trip; 6lb below last winning mark
runners.owner: Cheung Ming Man Cheung Ying Ho Et Al

runners.name: Whizz Kid
runners.spotlight: Reserve; seven wins over 5f including five here; 4lb lower than for the latest, but will need to perform better than of late in order to capitalise.
runners.comment: Five wins over C&D; 4lb below last winning mark, but will need to run better than of late

runners.name: Hey Bros
runners.spotlight: 2-7 in Australia; not beaten far on his Hong Kong debut over C&D last month, but not so good over 6f here three weeks ago; needs more.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on Hong Kong debut over C&D last month; not so good over 6f here next time
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Pang Wai Hak Pang Ka Yeung & Andy Pang Ka Wai

runners.name: Horsepower
runners.spotlight: Won three of his first four starts in Hong Kong including two over C&D; latest effort on dirt can be forgiven and he's back off his last winning mark, but he has an awkward draw.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; back off his last winning mark, but he has an awkward draw
runners.owner: Michael T H Lee Vanessa Lee & Amelia Lee

runners.name: Bunta Baby
runners.spotlight: Record over C&D since November reads 2131; 7lb rise for latest win and the return to Class 3 level will demand more, but still hard to dismiss in his current mood.
runners.comment: Record over C&D since November reads 2131; back up in class, but still hard to dismiss
runners.owner: Chu Tak Wing Casey Chu Yin Ling & Ronald Chu Tak Wah

runners.name: Youthful Spirits
runners.spotlight: Front-runner and dual C&D winner who is back off the same mark as for the latest in October; well drawn, but may face competition for the lead.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner back off last winning mark, but he may face competition for the lead

runners.name: Mr Desira
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won twice here (5f/6f) in the autumn, but has found life tougher at Class 3 level since; off three months and although he landed one of his four barrier trials, he still makes less appeal than a few.
runners.comment: Front-runner who won twice here in the autumn, but has found life tougher at Class 3 level

runners.name: Central Bank
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 2-7 in Australia and a couple of his local trials weren't without promise, but he finished near the back on last month's official Hong Kong debut at Sha Tin (5f); needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Finished near the back on last month's Hong Kong debut at Sha Tin; needs plenty more

runners.name: Copper Core
runners.spotlight: 1-2 in Australia; has gradually improved in his trials, but will need a good deal more in order to make a winning official Hong Kong debut.
runners.comment: Gradually improved in his trials, but will need plenty more on official Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Dashing Basics
runners.spotlight: 2-5 in Australia; landed his latest barrier trial on dirt at Sha Tin, but this looks a much stiffer task.
runners.comment: Landed his latest barrier trial on dirt at Sha Tin, but this looks a much stiffer task

runners.name: Together We Value
runners.spotlight: 2-9 in Australia; just beaten on his Hong Kong debut at Sha Tin (5f) last month, but not built on it in two subsequent starts and the outside stall is a real problem.
runners.comment: Just beaten on Hong Kong debut at Sha Tin last month, but not built on it; drawn widest
runners.owner: 25/26 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Candlelight Dinner
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; all three wins in Hong Kong have come over C&D; combination of 6f and a high draw found him out here four weeks ago, but he remains 7lb above his last winning mark and an uncontested lead seems unlikely.
runners.comment: All three wins in Hong Kong have come over C&D; still 7lb above his last winning mark

runners.name: Causeway King
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over 6f in New Zealand and makes his official Hong Kong debut after nine months off; made all in one of his barrier trials over C&D, but he faces a stiff task from his draw.
runners.comment: 2-3 in New Zealand; landed one his barrier trials, but faces a stiff task from his draw

races.name: Kwai Chung Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf) (Div II)

runners.name: Vigor Elleegant
runners.spotlight: Managed a Class 5 C&D win towards end of last season, but his recent form has been very underwhelming and he's drawn wide today.
runners.comment: Recent form has been very underwhelming and he's drawn wide today

runners.name: Call To Command
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden; kept on for third over C&D three weeks ago but quickly moves back up in grade today and is not ideally drawn.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D this month but quickly moves back up in grade today

runners.name: Motor
runners.spotlight: Made winning seasonal debut in first-time blinkers at Sha Tin in September and was placed there on next outing, but has lost his way since; others in this line-up have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Made good start to current campaign but has lost his way since

runners.name: Awesome Treasure
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D for David Hayes in December and was fourth here on last month's stable debut, but his latest run was underwhelming; double-digit draw further reduces his appeal today.
runners.comment: Fourth over C&D on stable debut but held since and has tough draw today

runners.name: Same To You
runners.spotlight: Reserve; did not kick on after winning seasonal debut at Sha Tin in September, but kept on for third off today's mark over C&D a fortnight ago and will enter calculations if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: Kept on for third over C&D a fortnight ago and competes off same mark today
runners.owner: Colin Cheung Kwok Lam Amos Cheung Sai Keung & Gary H K Yau

runners.name: Brave Win
runners.spotlight: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after seven starts; much better drawn than for last two runs but will probably be of greater interest when eligible for Class 5 races.
runners.comment: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after seven starts
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Matthew Lam Kin Hong

runners.name: Leading Dragon
runners.spotlight: Reserve; showed early speed and significant promise on all three 5f starts; will be trying a new trip if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: Showed early speed and significant promise on all three 5f starts; up in trip today

runners.name: Genius Baby
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D in December and has made the frame on three of his four starts since, including a close second from stall 10 here three weeks ago; much better drawn today; ought to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: In the frame on three of four starts since C&D win in December and has good draw today
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Pang Wai Hak Frankie Pang Ka Yeung & A Pang Ka Wai

runners.name: Silver Spurs
runners.spotlight: Placed twice since holding on well to make all over C&D in October; last month's C&D defeat (from stall 12) is not viewed harshly but his current mark probably demands a career-best performance.
runners.comment: Largely in good form this season but current mark probably demands a career-best effort

runners.name: Amazing Fun
runners.spotlight: Went very close off a reduced mark at Sha Tin (7f) in January, but two very disappointing runs over that trip have followed and he returns to sprinting with it all to prove again.
runners.comment: Nearly exploited a reduced mark in January but two very disappointing runs have followed

runners.name: Dan Attack
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner for new stable this season and still in good form; does not have much margin for error off current mark but, from stall 4, he probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner for new stable this season and still in good form; in the mix again

runners.name: Invincible Starr
runners.spotlight: Safely held on all four Hong Kong starts (5f-6f); benefits from a drop in grade today but the draw has not been kind.
runners.comment: Safely held on all four Hong Kong starts; eased in grade today but has a tough draw

runners.name: King Profit
runners.spotlight: 0-9 in Hong Kong but has posted some good efforts, most recently when second from stall 10 at Sha Tin last month; ideally drawn to attack from the front today; ought to give this a good shot.
runners.comment: Second from wide draw at Sha Tin last month; ideally drawn to attack from the front here
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs William Y W Lai Stanley Y C Hong & Sammy Y C Long

runners.name: Hayday
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner; did well to finish second from stall 10 here in January and last month's Sha Tin fifth was perfectly respectable; well drawn today; good chance.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; second from stall 10 on latest visit here; much better drawn today

races.name: Kwai Chung Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf) (Div III)

runners.name: California Deeply
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly when seventh over C&D a fortnight ago but this 6yo is seemingly on the downgrade; others have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly here a fortnight ago but is seemingly on the downgrade; others preferred

runners.name: Loving Vibes
runners.spotlight: Made encouraging stable debut over 5f at Sha Tin this month and will be suited by return to this C&D, but his outside draw is a big negative.
runners.comment: Made encouraging stable debut this month but outside draw a big negative today

runners.name: Master Of Humor
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all six starts; belied triple-digit odds when keeping on for respectable sixth over C&D last month, but finished last when tried over 1m three weeks ago; overlooked, despite favourable draw.
runners.comment: Unplaced all six starts and overlooked, despite favourable draw

runners.name: Giant Leap
runners.spotlight: Four-time C&D winner, most recently in higher-grade company last April; not firing on all cylinders this season but kept on for second behind Giant Ballon off his much reduced mark here three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Not at best this season but kept on for second off his reduced mark here this month

runners.name: Brownneedsfurther
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden; well beaten over 7f at Sha Tin last month, but had a tough draw that day and has claims here if judged on his close third over 6f there two starts ago; sports first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Had tough draw when well beaten over 7f last time; went close over 6f two starts ago

runners.name: Crystal Powerful
runners.spotlight: Four-time course winner (5f-6f); on a good mark now but has become very inconsistent and dropped through the field very tamely after racing prominently from the inside stall here last time; back from 11-week break.
runners.comment: On a good mark but has become very inconsistent; returns from 11-week break

runners.name: Kwai Chung Talents
runners.spotlight: Bettered inauspicious debut (5f) when midfield over C&D last month but is probably one for a bit further down the line.
runners.comment: Second run was better than his debut but he needs to take another big step forward
runners.owner: Rotary Club Of Kwai Chung Syndicate

runners.name: Starry Show
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten at triple-digit odds here a fortnight ago and has now been unplaced in all six Hong Kong starts.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten at triple-digit odds here a fortnight ago and now 0-6 in Hong Kong

runners.name: Victor The Rapid
runners.spotlight: In-form 6yo who has been placed on both starts since last month's C&D win; nicely drawn in stall 3 today and will probably be in the shake-up again.
runners.comment: In-form 6yo who has been placed on both starts since last month's C&D win

runners.name: Giant Ballon
runners.spotlight: Posted string of good efforts before dominating from the front and winning comfortably over C&D three weeks ago; open to more improvement after only eight starts but has 9lb rise and double-digit draw to contend with.
runners.comment: Dominated from the front here last month; 9lb higher and drawn wide today
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs George Chang And George Hing Chang

runners.name: Phoenix Light
runners.spotlight: Yet to hit top gear after three runs for new stable this year, albeit he encountered some traffic on last two occasions, and has another double-digit draw today; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top gear for new stable this year and has another double-digit draw

runners.name: Lucky Together
runners.spotlight: Reserve; ex-Australian 4yo who is yet to make a splash after five Hong Kong starts (6f-7f); will appreciate this ease in grade but still needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Ex-Australian 4yo who is yet to make a splash after five Hong Kong starts

runners.name: The Perfect Match
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who returned from a layoff with three creditable C&D runs towards end of last year and probably lost his chance when racing very wide into the home straight here in January; not ideally drawn but continues to edge down the weights and might be the answer.
runners.comment: In the frame on three of his four starts this season and continues to edge down ratings

runners.name: Sky Cap
runners.spotlight: Reserve; held on well to score over C&D a week ago and remains well handicapped on older form, but has become too inconsistent to rely upon heavily.
runners.comment: Held on well for C&D win a week ago but is too inconsistent to rely upon heavily

races.name: Fanling Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Daryl Flash
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won three times when based in Australia but began Hong Kong career with four low-key runs (6f-1m) and probably needs more help from the local handicapper.
runners.comment: Safely held all four HK starts; probably needs more help from local handicapper

runners.name: California Moxie
runners.spotlight: Won more cosily than narrow margin suggests here three weeks ago, his fourth C&D success; 6lb rise enforces this rise in grade but Zac Purton is booked again; no surprise if he's up to the task.
runners.comment: Up in grade today but won more cosily than narrow margin suggests here this month

runners.name: Fluorescence
runners.spotlight: Well down the field at triple-digit odds on both Hong Kong starts (7f/1m); blinkers removed after one use.
runners.comment: Well down the field at triple-digit odds on both Hong Kong starts

runners.name: Positive Smile
runners.spotlight: Struck form in January, winning twice over 7f in Class 4; not disgraced when upped to this grade last month but needs to find something extra today and is not yet proven over 1m.
runners.comment: Dual 7f Class 4 winner in January; held in this grade since, though, and unproven over 1m

runners.name: Keefy
runners.spotlight: Not in peak form this season but handicapped accordingly now and has made the frame here (1m/1m1f) on both starts since cheekpieces were added last month; tried in a visor today; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Made the frame in cheekpieces here on last two outings; visor given a whirl today

runners.name: Without Compare
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo who has made the frame on all four Hong Kong starts (7f-1m; all at Sha Tin); can probably win soon but has awkward draw for a prominent racer.
runners.comment: In the frame all four Hong Kong starts but has awkward draw today

runners.name: Thriving Brothers
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent since joining current stable just under a year ago; switches back to turf after very respectable dirt debut ten days ago and has slipped to a good mark; considered, despite double-digit draw.
runners.comment: Largely consistent since joining current stable and has slipped to a good mark
runners.owner: Yeung Chun Wang Yeung Ho Wang & Yeung Chung Wang

runners.name: King Lotus
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f here on seasonal debut in September and has made the frame over C&D three times since, but his last two runs were very disappointing and he now has a point to prove.
runners.comment: Began season in good form but has a point to prove after two down-the-field runs

runners.name: Fortunate Son
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual C&D winner in Class 4 this season; gave very good account when fourth in this grade last month, but two down-the-field finishes followed and he'll probably remain vulnerable at this level.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner in Class 4 this season but recent efforts have been disappointing

runners.name: Beauty Alliance
runners.spotlight: Back up to a career-high mark today, but ran commendably well when close second in Class 2 event at Sha Tin (1m1f) this month and is well drawn; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Ran commendably well when close second at Sha Tin this month and has good draw here

runners.name: Trinity Treasure
runners.spotlight: Made winning debut in 6f maiden in Australia last June; moves up in trip for Hong Kong debut; market may guide.
runners.comment: Absent since winning a 6f maiden in Australia on debut last June; market may guide
runners.owner: Zhao Guoming Xiao Bing Annie An Ran & Tan Meng Sha

runners.name: Solid Win
runners.spotlight: In career-best form this year, winning twice over C&D; went up 8lb for his 2l success four weeks ago but must be considered in current mood.
runners.comment: In career-best form this year, winning twice over C&D; one to consider

runners.name: Flying Fortress
runners.spotlight: Went close over C&D on consecutive appearances towards end of last year, but has not been in quite the same form lately and remains on a fairly tough mark.
runners.comment: Not quite at best lately and remains on fairly tough mark
runners.owner: 23/24 Cody Mo Wai Kit Trainer Syndicate

races.name: Kwu Tung Handicap (Class 3) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Sugar Sugar
runners.spotlight: Seven-time C&D winner; inconsistent this season but was close third to My Day My Way here two starts ago and remains on a workable mark; can't be ruled out from stall 2.
runners.comment: Seven C&D wins; inconsistent nowadays but is on workable mark and has good draw

runners.name: Charming Babe
runners.spotlight: Gained both wins in Class 4; did well to finish fifth in this grade here last month, having encountered some traffic, but was soundly beaten on latest outing and is not the percentage call today.
runners.comment: Did well to finish fifth here last month but soundly beaten since; others look stronger

runners.name: Iron Security
runners.spotlight: Ex-Autralian 4yo; safely held on first two Hong Kong starts (5f/6f); favourable draw not enough to make him of interest .
runners.comment: Ex-Autralian 4yo; safely held on first two Hong Kong starts (5f/6f)
runners.owner: Jimmy Tang Kwok Leung Ho Kai Man & Ricky Lee Wing Ngai

runners.name: Copartner Fleet
runners.spotlight: Edged ahead close home to score over 5f here in January, but was safely held over 6f at Sha Tin when back up in this higher grade last month and, on balance, others look stronger.
runners.comment: 5f course winner in January but found things tougher when back in this higher grade

runners.name: Metro Power
runners.spotlight: Just did enough to make all over C&D a fortnight ago; evidently in good nick but combination of wide draw and career-high mark asks a lot of him.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D this month but has tough draw and career-high mark to overcome now

runners.name: Pegas
runners.spotlight: Scored at Sha Tin in January, his third Hong Kong start, and also ran well when fourth over C&D three weeks ago; well drawn today and may yet have a bigger run in him; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Sha Tin winner in January and ran well here this month; not yet fully exposed in Hong Kong

runners.name: California Blitz
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with two C&D wins and has been placed over C&D in all three starts this year, most recently when close third from stall 12 last month; likely to give another good account today but has same tough draw again.
runners.comment: Very creditable third from stall 12 here last month, but has same tough draw again

runners.name: Yee Cheong Glory
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who has been back in good form this year, most recently when winning in good style at Sha Tin 17 days ago; moves up in grade here but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Moves up in grade after winning in pretty good style at Sha Tin this month
runners.owner: Choi Hiu Fung & Choi Yu So Fong

runners.name: Amazing Kid
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to score over C&D in this grade three weeks ago and has now won four times this season; another 7lb higher today but it's hard to tell when his improvement will level off.
runners.comment: Very progressive 4yo who has collected four wins this season; must be considered

runners.name: Beauty Glory
runners.spotlight: Reserve; went very close over C&D two starts ago and had tough draw when only eighth here three weeks ago, but younger rivals appeal more all the same.
runners.comment: Went very close here two starts ago but younger rivals appeal more all the same

runners.name: Symbol Of Strength
runners.spotlight: Came good in Hong Kong at ninth attempt, scoring by a neck over C&D three weeks ago; 6lb higher today but has pretty good draw and further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Came good in HK over C&D this month and further progress is possible

runners.name: Greater Bae
runners.spotlight: Displayed improvement to win cosliy over C&D in January, his third Hong Kong start, and endured a wide trip when onlly fifth off new mark here last month; Zac Purton keeps the ride; remains of strong interest.
runners.comment: Cosy C&D winner in January; endured wide trip when fifth last time; remains of interest

runners.name: My Day My Way
runners.spotlight: Caused 29-1 surprise over C&D last month and now has 2-11 strike-rate, but needs to kick on again to win this competitive race from stall 10.
runners.comment: Surprise C&D winner last month but needs to kick on again to overcome stall 10 today

runners.name: Celestial Hero
runners.spotlight: Reserve; needs a good pace to aim at but has been back in good form this year, making the frame at Sha Tin on his last three outings; no surprise if he appears late on the scene.
runners.comment: Back in good form this year; no surprise if he appears late on the scene
runners.owner: Dr David Chain Chi Woo Bernard Chain S Chiang & C C S Liang
